title: support resizing by grabbing bounding box edge
body:
No reason restricting resizing only to handles. In fact, we can remove the n/e/s/w handles if support this.

The three main use cases are:

1. Element is too large and the handles are off-screen
2. Element is too small and we don't show the handles
3. Improve resizing UX in general

Figma:

![figma-resize-by-edge](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/147880325-37d575cd-996c-4463-9b0d-19264e173a8f.gif)

title: [Bug] Inconsistent trackpad input on Ipad
body:
## Details
Trackpad input inconsistently recognized by excalidraw on iPad.

## Environment: 
- iOS 15.1 (2020 iPad Pro 12.9")
- Excalidraw v1.5.16 in Obsidian v1.0.5
- excalidraw.com
- Logitech Combo Touch iPad Pro (5th gen)

## Video
https://www.dropbox.com/s/rhoy7r7lfdhb8i5/excalidraw_obsidian_ipad_single_click_not_recognized.mp4?dl=0

### Demo steps
* In Obsidian: 
  * Using trackpad, select rectangle, try to draw a rectangle (cannot draw) 
  * Using finger, select diamond, draw a diamond
  * Using trackpad, try to deselect diamond by single clicking on canvas (cannot deselect)
  * Using trackpad, double-click and can input text, can click off of text
  * Using trackpad, try to select text then diamond (cannot select)
  * Using finger, select text
* In excalidraw.com / safari:
  * Using trackpad, try to select rectangle (cannot select) 
  * Using finger, select diamond, draw a diamond
  * Using trackpad, try to deselect diamond by single clicking on canvas (cannot deselect)
  * Using trackpad, double-click and can input text, can click off of text
  * Using trackpad, try to select text then diamond (cannot select)
  * Using trackpad, click on new tab, click on google, click on search, click back to excalidraw
  * Using trackpad, can now select rectangle, but still cannot draw

author: aur3l14no

title: Proposal to ditch the current dark mode and opt for a better one
body:
Hi. I have been using Excalidraw almost exclusively whenever I need to draw, explain, or just mess around with shapes. I absolutely love it, and the fact that it's open source. But throughout the period during which I used it I quickly started noticing a lot of issues with the current "Dark Mode". Basically #2346, #3531, #3606, #4074, and #4301. I'd like to list these issues and the possible fixes for them in this proposal. 

## Here are my reasons:
1. It's *super* laggy on Firefox. Canvas filters are not the best in Firefox and they certainly lack the performance of, say, Chrome. Drawing a straight line using the Pen tool in Firefox with Dark Mode on runs at an average of 27 FPS, while drawing complicated circles and random shapes runs at an average of 56 FPS. (#4074 #2346)
2. Colours don't look that great. I draw a red line, I expect it to be red in Dark Mode, but it turns out something entirely different. Even custom hex codes don't work properly. (#3531)
3. Not to mention the various bugs with Safari not supporting svg invert and so on. 

I dug deeper and found out about the tiny `invert` and `hue-rotate` thingy, but that just doesn't work, given the previously mentioned issues. 

## My proposal
1. First off, rename the current Dark Mode to Invert Mode, with clear warnings of what might the output and bugs be. 
2. Then, create a new Dark Mode that only changes the colour of the controls and UI without touching the rendering canvas itself. Maybe change the background colour as well, just for the sake of consistency and user expectations. This would be a true dark mode experience that is completely lag free (I tested it!), respects browser limitations (also tested it!), and doesn't suffer any of the aforementioned issues. 

I currently have Excalidraw with a custom background of the value `#121212` (dark grey) that is consistent with [Material Design's dark theme](https://material.io/design/color/dark-theme.html#properties) (which most users are familiar with), and it's quite comfortable. I didn't face any of the issues mentioned above or other issues regarding dark mode. 

> I'm willing to work on this, just point me in the right direction.

title: Rvert persistent alt/option-duplication
body:
At some point we've decided to make pressing option/alt even one permanently duplicate the element, even if you release the modifier before you release the pointer (`pointerup`). The reason was to fix cases where you release both the modifier and pointer around the same time and you often release in the wrong order, ending up in not duplicating. We've decided it was worth the UX improvement to go against the standard.

But, I've noticed that Figma (recently?) moved to this behavior: delayed reversal. If you release the modifier, set a timeout (~1sec) before the duplication is reversed. I think this is a great middle ground, and we should do the same.

Benefits of allowing to revert are:

1. user decides to not duplicate while still dragging
2. we can overload the alt modifier (e.g. for https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/2903 etc.)

author: jpy-git

title: Feature: Grid units for scaled drawings
body:
I would like to associate a value and unit with the grid so that I can sketch scaled drawings with some accuracy.

**Problem**
Excalidraw is a fun tool for creating basic elevations and/or floor plans. The grid + the "stats for nerds" panel make it "possible" to produce scaled drawings but some simple enhancements would make it a lot easier. Currently I maintain a spreadsheet that converts my measurements to the 20px units used by the grid. 

**Proposed Solution**
Add a settings button to the "stats for nerds" panel and allow us to enter a numeric size for one square grid. Then on the "stats for nerds" panel convert the pixel measurements to grid size.

**Stretch Goal** 
Provide the option to format the size value. For example if I have set my grid size to 3 (inches) I would like to provide a format for displaying sizes in imperial, where value is the size in inches (not the number of grid units): {floor(value/12)}' {mod(value/12)}"

Additionally you could also provide presets: 
Imperial - Inches as Whole Feet Inches
Imperial - Inches as Decimal Feet
Imperial - Feet as Decimal Feet
Metric ...

author: zsviczian

title: Freehand pen mode skips stroke if it starts within ? ms after previous stroke
body:
Before I start, I would like to mention that I'm really happy with the new pen mode with palm rejection. It's a major improvement over the previous situation!

I tried hand writing in freehand mode with an Apple Pencil (i.e., "drawing" text, not using the Pencil as an alternative keyboard). I'm seeing this issue both in mobile Safari and in the mobile Obsidian app through the Excalidraw plugin for Obsidian (which I updated to the latest version before testing).

To reproduce, it might be sufficient to try hand-writing a sentence with the freehand draw tool using any hardware stylus.

When hand writing, I (and probably most people) make quick movements with the pen. Sometimes I write subsequent letters in a single stroke (ligatures), while other times, I lift the pen briefly between two letters in order to start the next letter with a new stroke (non-ligatures). When I write a non-ligature within a word, Excalidraw ignores the second stroke. After that it will pick up the next (i.e., third) stroke again. This has the overall effect of skipping every second stroke within a word.

Rather than an even-odd problem, it seems to be a timing issue (i'm guessing `debounce` or `throttle`, but I will refrain from interpreting the issue). If I intentionally wait a little between two strokes, both strokes will be picked up. The first stroke of a new word is also always picked up, which seems to suggest that the slightly longer break introduced by a space (which is typically when I shift my hand a bit) is just enough to prevent the next stroke from being ignored.

While the new pen mode works very well for me in general, this stroke-skipping issue is currently preventing me from using Excalidraw for the purpose I have in mind.

author: dodalovic

title: Embedded Excalidraw component not getting keyboard focus on touch, but only on click
body:
I received the following user feedback: https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/426
In short, when using pen or touch input, keyboard shortcuts do not work until the user clicks the canvas with the mouse.

On Excalidraw.com this is not an issue since the keyboard event listener is attached to the document object. Excalidraw Obsidian has `handleKeyboardGlobally: false`; 

I understand why in the case of `handleKeyboardGlobally === false` you'd not want to attach the listener to document

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7a6119646270a03ae68fb364d9b13e87227e5a80/src/components/App.tsx#L991-L993

However, I do not understand why you also do not want to attach it to excalidraw-container:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7a6119646270a03ae68fb364d9b13e87227e5a80/src/components/App.tsx#L475-L477

That said, changing the above to `onKeyDown={this.onKeyDown}` has not really resulted in any noticeable difference with regards to the focus issue.

Two things solved the issue for me:
1) setting `props.autoFocus = true;` Why is this not true by default?
2) changing `handleCanvasPointerDown` by adding `this.focusContainer();` to the function. Again, I've seen no negative effects, but now if I touch the canvas it receives focus and all the shortcuts work as expected

Do you have anything against adding focusContainer() to handleCanvasPointerDown in master?
From this:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7a6119646270a03ae68fb364d9b13e87227e5a80/src/components/App.tsx#L2748-L2751

to this:

```typescript
  private handleCanvasPointerDown = (
    event: React.PointerEvent<HTMLCanvasElement>,
  ) => {
    this.focusContainer();
    // remove any active selection when we start to interact with canvas 
```

author: sugoidesune

title: Arrows shouldn't bind to any shapes
body:
![output](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/75205/154266289-96bdd9ce-92c9-49c3-a3be-b25fe6ba850f.gif)


I love Excalidraw low fidelity approach, but the arrow bindings are driving me insane. Arrows locks to _everything_, and as you edit text, resize elements, move elements, the arrows _always_ end up wrong once the operation is complete.

Related to #4324, I want to suggest that arrows should not bind to any shapes _at all_, and let them behave like any other object, rather than _trying_ to be smart about it.

Count the "time saved" by arrows locking to objects v.s "time lost" by arrows locking to objects, at least for me that would be something like seconds saved and hours lost when aggregated.

title: Perfect Freehand Drawing
body:
> This issue has been moved over from the [Obsidian Excalidraw Plugin](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/440)

Handwriting using Freedraw has now become feasible due to the palm rejection feature, however I still found handwriting to feel awkward and unreliable on iPad. The smoothing feels way too strong, and I get too many errors (for example the top two points of a `U` will often connect). I tried this [perfect-freehand demo](https://perfect-freehand-example-steveruizok.vercel.app) ([GitHub](https://github.com/steveruizok/perfect-freehand)), and immediately found it way more comfortable to use. It would be great if Excalidraw would be able to offer support for a similar style of freedraw, or an option to manually adjust the smoothing of the pen to make it more comfortable!

Weirdly the [demo linked on the GitHub ](https://perfect-freehand-example.vercel.app) does not feel as great as the demo I've linked above 🤔

title: android app workflow to open and save drawings?
body:
I am trying to use the excalidraw Android app and to find an easy way to work with files in folders other than "download".

- I can only save a drawing using the "export" feature.
- this feature allows only to enter a filename and to press "save as file"
- this will save the file in the download folder, and I can't see a way to select a different folder.
- now I need to use a file explorer to copy or move the drawing to the target folder, for example to a working folder of a cloned git repository, so I can take it under source control

I am looking for a way:

- to open the file from a specific location (this is possible)
- to "save" and to "save as", like I would do in a text editor.  
But it looks like the only way is to export the file again, then it will be a new copy in the downloads folder and so on.
- I think this workflow is complicated.
- it would also be fine if the file extension could be associated with the app so it would be possible to use a file explorer to locate drawings and to just open the files from there in the app.

title: Idea: Save/Open drawings embedded in image files (e g mydiagram.excalidraw.png )
body:
# Idea: Save/Open drawings embedded in image files (e g mydiagram.excalidraw.png )

Similar to what draw.io / diagrams.net does - make it so that excalidraw can save and open files locally as embedded image files, i e: The excalidraw drawing is embedded into e g the `.png` or `.svg` file. And conversely, excalidraw can open these files.

It makes for several benefits: 
* Adding drawings / images to a git repo, and referring to them in .md files, without having to bother with a separate source file for the drawing.
* Thumbnails. Most file UI navigating environments will show you a thumbnail of the `png`.
* A really simple way to store drawings, and ensure that the drawing source is not separated from the image.
etc

author: dwelle

title: non-atomic erasing for linear & freedraw shapes
body:
Instead of removing whole shapes, with linear and freedraw shapes we can erase points that are close in proximity to the cursor.

@maielo pointed out this behavior may actually be undesirable for most use cases that excalidraw is used for, where deleting whole shapes is faster than being forced to erase all individual points. So if we implement this we should make this opt-in from the properties pannel.

Spec:

- it should split the shape into two when a middle point is removed
- for better results, we should create new points at the edges of the split lines to minimize the effect on the resulting shape

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/157909221-e438e53e-96c3-4943-b704-4e6edb2d602c.png)

Ultimately, a solution that creates even more points to better approximate the original shape would be ideal, but not easy:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/157909557-69e49202-f871-4c1f-8a29-c5173da859d5.png)

title: Support custom elements
body:
Spec

- `{ type: "custom", svgContent: '...' }`
- custom rendering function `renderCustomElement(element: ExcalidrawElement): void` that will be called on each render loop
- should support disabling resizing/rotation
- should support binding on other excalidraw elements (dropping the custom element on top of another element should bind to it so that that dragging the parent element will move the bound custom element as well)
- `onPointerDown(element: ExcalidrawElement, eventData: { ... })`, `onPointerUp(element: ExcalidrawElement, eventData: { ... })`  handler
	- technically, we could put the handler on the custom element itself — but it could be hard making it work with how the editor is designed right now (around serialization etc.). So we'll need to start with having the callback on the editor component-level
- an easy way for the host app to get editor coords, and/or subscribe to coords changes (scene coords + viewport/offsets), so that if the host app renders custom UI on top of canvas (such as one that's supposed to be visually aligned to the custom element), it's position can be synced properly to the canvas
author: dwelle

title: Hole in a shape
body:
I found no way to make a shape with an hole in excalidraw. 

For example consider a square with a circle inside it; the region between the circle and the square is, say, yellow, while the circle let you to see what is on the lower layers. I was trying to do something like that for a magnifier icon that do not cover what is inside the "Lens".

Drawing what is under the magnifier obove it, cutting it to make it appear as it was under the lens, is not really a solution, both becaust it take a very long time, both because in this way it would not be exportable as a library object. 

I see that the boolean operation were rejected (issue #1909 and pull request #1905). I understand that excalidraw aims to an extreme user friendly interface, and probably I agree with you that a fully operational boolean operation is too much for it. But maybe another simpler way may be found to just support holes? The result of the other boolean operations can be obtained with other methods, but as I sayd I found no way to obtain an holed shape.

Is there some way to do it right now? If not, could it be a good addition for excalidraw?

title: Object rotation broken in Chrome
body:
Rotating an object in Chrome (tested with Version: 99.0.4844.83) always snaps object to weird angles.

When I create a rectangle and rotate it without holding shift, it snaps to these angles:
27 deg, 45 deg, 63 deg, 90 deg, 135 deg, 153 deg, 180 deg, 207 deg, 225 deg, 243 deg, 270 deg, 315 deg, 0 deg

When I do the same in Safari (Version 15.4 (17613.1.17.1.6)), I can freely rotate the object to any desired angle (which is what I would expect when not holding shift).

When holding shift in Chrome the angles are also not as fine-granular as in Safari; there I can rotate an object in increments of 15 deg, in Chrome some of these angles are skipped.

title: Wrapped text is not correctly centered in boxes
body:
When a text goes multiline automatically because of its length, the centering algorithm keeps the trailing whitespaces in its calculation of each line width.  
As a result, lines that end with a space because the next word was wrapped are shifted to the left.

You can see in the example below the same boxes with almost identical texts:
- Left one is the uninterrupted sentence "Some text that will wrap automatically"
- Right one is "Some text that`\r`will wrap`\r`automatically"

You can see that the text is correctly centered only in the second instance:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/314079/160939905-c9bc0c55-1627-439c-800a-0deddf81118c.png)

<small>(pixel measurements are indicative, just used the screenshot tool on macOS at 100% zoom on Excalidraw)</small>

Depending on the text, it can have more "dramatic" effect; here the left box text looks achored to the right:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/314079/160940271-ebf8aa2b-cfab-4899-a762-869826be01a7.png)
author: olance

title: Duplicated shapes with inner text use mixed z-index
body:
When a shape is double-clicked to add text in it, and then duplicated, the text & shape elements of both instances do not seem to be using the same z-index.
Instead, both text elements are sorted higher higher than both shapes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/314079/160941854-0461fecb-a20e-43a3-a461-cd1e69a6e547.png)

- In this image, both shapes have a solid white background.
- The red shape and its text were created first
- The blue shape is a duplicate of the red one
- Without further manipulation, you can see that the blue shape does cover the red one (i.e. red borders are not visible through the blue shape's background) but the red text is not affected by the blue shape's background
- If I then select the red shape and choose "move to back", it gives the expected result:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/314079/160942311-9bae8226-e526-4621-b8a1-505c4b5bca5d.png)

- Duplicating one of the shapes again will show the same issue:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/314079/160942423-330a6c91-d6c6-47a1-9921-100d650ce8fd.png)

- But creating a new shape will not:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/314079/160942621-6fd1cabb-eaf2-4eca-abfd-e19316d09d88.png)

And, finally, copying and then pasting a shape will not show the issue either.

title: Bind label to arrow
body:
Original issue raised by @Dcarnik for the Obsidian-Excalidarw plugin ([#570](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/570))

The arrow/line which connects two figures follows the figure when figure is moved to other position. That is great! We use text to identify the line (for process diagram), like numbers or other text parameters. In case the text is placed next to the line, after moving the figure, the line follows the  figure, yet text stays in same position (see fig. 1, 2)  

It would be nice that text moves together with lane (see fig. 3). 

The ideal solution. The line/arrow when double clicked allows to add text which is then connected with line/arrow. The line/arrow separates into 2 parts (see fig. 4). In case text manually moved to other position which do not intersects line, the line/arrow's 2 segments become as 1 segment. 
Good realization of that was said above at: https://www.diagrams.net/ 
Thanks.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/57209269/161514050-c0f60359-f422-4501-8e76-924aec14e424.png)

author: MrSatco

title: bind c to ellipse
body:
Is there any reason *not* to bind the `c` key to the ellipse tool? With the addition of the eraser, ellipses can no longer be created using the mnemonic `e` key. It's very nice that excalidraw uses mnemonic keyboard shortcuts all on the left side of a qwerty keyboard. The use of `o` for oval is fine but `o` is on the right side of the keyboard. Why not let users use `c` for circle since it's mnemonic, easy to remember, and on the left side of a qwerty keyboard. 

I understand the ellipse tool does not draw circles by default but does it really matter? we're not teaching users the difference between circles and ovals we're providing mnemonic left-handed keyboard shortcuts so that users can switch tools without taking their hands off of the mouse.

title: Typescript type files are missing from NPM package.
body:
It seems like there are some `.d.ts` files in your repo (e.g. `global.d.ts`) that aren't installed by `npm install @excalidraw/excalidraw`. They contain types that are referenced by the code (e.g. `MarkOptional`) so as soon as you `import Excalidraw from "@excalidraw/excalidraw"` you get `tsc` errors like this:

```
node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/types/actions/types.d.ts:8:16 - error TS2304: Cannot find name 'MarkOptional'.

8     appState?: MarkOptional<AppState, "offsetTop" | "offsetLeft" | "width" | "height"> | null;
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

I'm kind of surprised about this because it means you can't use Excalidraw in a Typescript project, and surely loads of people are doing that?

author: Sohailamjad1

title: exportToSvg() does not respect window.EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH
body:
Dear excalidraw team.

First of all, thank you for your great efforts. I am working on integrating the excalidraw library into the trilium note app (https://github.com/zadam/trilium/pull/2798). Since trilium is mainly self-hosted, it is important to self-host all assets.

After setting `window.EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH` it seemed to solve the issue when using the react version. However, since we use `exportToSvg()` to save an SVG-image, I found, that some assets are still loaded from a 3rd party. This time not unpkg.com, but excalidraw.com. 

I did not have time to do a solution, but I found the lines that indicates the problem.

- https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/c5a7723185f6ca05e0ceb0b0d45c4e3fbcb81b2a/src/scene/export.ts#L125
- https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/c5a7723185f6ca05e0ceb0b0d45c4e3fbcb81b2a/src/scene/export.ts#L129

Thank you for your time.

Best regards
Thomas

PS: Of course, we could just string-replace the url of the fonts, but it looked like a minor bug to me.

author: SymFlorian

title: Highlight words in a paragraph
body:
Hello I don't know if I should drop this idea here but I really wish this feature would become true.

Currently we can't highlight words in a paragraph.
That can be really annoying because it keeps me from making my text more readable.
There are some work arounds with some dividers for example but it's not ideal in my opinion.

It'd be really nice if we could make some words bold, or even colorize some words in a paragraph instead of all of it.
author: YummyGyros

title: Disable updating locked elements
body:
When an element is locked, I think we should disable any kind of update (eg changing background, stroke, etc). Right now we disable interaction (eg moving, resizing) but we should also disable any kind of properties update as the element is locked.

Figjam, Miro, whimisical as well don't allow updating elements unless unlocked.

As a user, I think locking means "Disable any kind of updates related to element" and not just updates related to element dimensions/position
author: ad1992

title: Enable selection of single elements  to unlock when locked for better UX
body:
As per UX, I feel it's not intuitive to disable selection and I am not sure why we are disabling selection, even when you right-click the element it gets selected.
As per the flow, I select an element -> right-click, -> lock, hence I am expecting then when I click on an element again it will select it, and then I can either use the context menu/shortcut to unlock it.
Miro, Figjam , whimisical  allow the selection of lock elements and show a visual indicator to unlock as well which makes it more intuitive.
Hence I would suggest we allow selection and show some indicator (eg lock icon) that makes it clear that the element is locked so the user needs to unlock it(clicking on lock icon will unlock the element).

And this is most helpful during collaboration where users don't know which elements are locked and which aren't
And one more thing, disabling selection+ not showing indicator might look like a bug when someone tries to interact with the elements (eg move, resize) unless the user is aware of the lock functionality in Excalidraw

In case we want to disable selection then I suggest showing some indicator that makes it more clear how to unlock and why the selection is disabled but to align with other tools I think we should allow selection + some indicator to unlock when selected as well.

author: dwelle

title: Text editing focus is lost if switching to and back from another browser tab/window/application, which hurts `usability`
body:
Text editing focus is lost if switching to and back from another browser tab/window/application, which hurts **usability**:

- start editing text and/or select some portion of text
- switch to another application to grab (copy) some additional text
- switch back to diagramm to paste the additional text

The editing focus is lost at that point, you need to do actions to select text to start editing again (for grouped elements that may take time).

body:
Hello @ Someone Amazing on the Team Excalidraw  !

Is it possible to improve the performance of the tool freedraw ? I've seen #4726 you have already reduced the number of dots in the ink, can this be continued (if that's the reason for the performance to many dots) as now with a large number of lines starts [hangs](https://1drv.ms/v/s!Aug1AU2reREylgkJReSTitEW-4vz?e=Xwqs2U) navigation, new lines appear with a delay, there is no such a thing in google keep. Thanks for the attention!

author: vivekbhg

title: Launching .excalidraw from file manager opens incorrect file in PWA
body:
When opening a *.excalidraw file from the windows file manager, the excalidraw PWA opens the wrong file. It launches PWA with the last open file active (not the launched file).

**Expected behaviour:** When opening a *.excalidraw file (eg by double click), the PWA should open and load that file.

### Steps to reproduce
Preconditions: PWA installed, file handling enabled.
1. Clear the canvas in excalidraw
2. Optional: Close all excalidraw  PWAs
3. Double click a saved *.excalidraw file (the file should have content)
4. PWA opens to blank canvas

### System
OS: Windows 11 22000.675
Browser: Edge 101.0.1210.47 
  File Handling API: Enabled
  File Handling Icons: Enabled

I can work around this by signing out on my ipad or using a browser that isn't logged in. I would rather not have to do this though and have it such that I can be logged in on both and see live updates from the other device.

title: Bug review

author: alexflint

title: "Copy styles" to include objects and its size
body:
I'm using the "[sticky notes](https://twitter.com/aakansha1216/status/1471509326674030592)" feature all the time and think that it could be even more helpful, if "copy styles" (ctrl + alt + c) could also include the object around the text and also its size. In this way, it would be possible to quickly adjust the size of several sticky notes (besides its color, fonts size, font type etc.) at once, without manually going through each of them and manually resizing the border. This would be especially useful on whiteboards with a lot of text boxes, formatted as sticky notes, to have automatic line breaks within rectangles or circles.

Take for instance [this](https://excalidraw.com/#room=9e0af8ca4fd74e2eab26,Cvt0J8geh6t8dqWpzoV45A) whiteboard. Under `1` you see a paragraph with several manual line breaks, to fit the text to a certain width, thus making it more readable. If you decide to alter the text, you would have to manually adjust the line breaks in order to fit the width, line by line. This is very tedious, but the sticky notes feature comes to the rescue, see `2`! If we make the rectangle transparent, see `3`, then the text magically auto wraps, which saves a lot of hassle. But how to apply the same width to other such text boxes? At the moment it's not possible, but with "copy styles" that will also include the size of this rectangle, we would finally have a solution (and many more happy Excalidraw users)!

So the ideal workflow would look like this:
1. create a sticky note, either with transparent or solid borders, and fill it with some text
2. copy its style
3. apply this style to a normal text on the whiteboard and this text should inherit all the elements the sticky note has, especially its size
4. write in this sticky note and rejoice with joy about the automatic line breaks
5. if you want to change the size of several sticky notes at once, copy the style of your preferred sticky note, select the sticky notes you want to change (by holding shift or selecting an area) and apply your copied style
6. …
7. profit!

author: mchoraine

title: keeping element references up to date
body:
### problem

This concerns mainly `appState` where we temporarily keep references to excalidraw elements, such as `draggingElement`, `editingElement`, `pendingImageElement` and so on.

We tend to update elements either by mutating them (`mutateElement()`) or by creating a new object, which retains immutability (`newElementWith()`). We use mutation mainly for performance reasons in tight loops. Disregarding performance, immutability is preferred in general.

But, when we create a new object while updating an element, it means the appState references are no longer pointing to the new element, which leads to reading/updating stale data no longer existing in the elements array.

We're working around this in ad hoc ways, sometimes updating the appState reference at the same time we update the element, or by treating the state as just an id reference and retrieving the actual element on each access. Sometimes we forget to do either and this leads to buggy behavior.

We should come up with a generic solution we don't need to thinking about in every case.

### proposals

1) migrate all state element references to ids so that we're forced to always retrieve latest elements.

	**Downsides:** worse DX, more boilerplate. Potentially slower speed. Necessity to pass `Scene` instance where we only pass `appState`, so that we can retrieve the element.

    (This is what I'm doing for now to fix https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5322)

2) (auto) update appState references inside when renewing element references.

	**Downsides:** Would require big rewrites in order for this to not have bigger effects on perf, and had limited effect on DX. Also not 100% safe as there's no guarantee we don't renew references in ways (history pop, ad-hoc object literal creation, ...).

3) ???

title: raf-throttle renderScene
body:
From testing in collab, but possibly even in singleplayer, rendering can often be called 1.5-2x+ more often than the framerate. We should throttle.

- [x] support `trailing: true` for `throttleRAF()`
- [x] export raf-throttled renderScene and use for rendering in `App.tsx`
author: dwelle

title: investigate batching collab socket events
body:
There are events that are usually emitted in tandem in a tight loop, namely `SCENE_UPDATE` and `MOUSE_LOCATION`. We emit and listen to them separately, which ends up sending more events, and updating state more often than necessary.

I propose we batch them where/if possible. That means that `MOUSE_LOCATION` wouldn't be emitted as `volatile` when batched with non-volatile events.
author: dwelle

title: introduce `UnrestoredExcalidrawElement` type
body:
It's unreasonable to assume we'll remember to `restore()` elements in all the necessary places, namely after we retrieve them from external boundaries. So let's typesafe it.

- [ ] make `ExcalidrawElement` into a branded type of `{ _brand: "RestoredExcalidrawElement }`
- [ ] add `UnrestoredExcalidrawElement`, and use it for all elements coming from external boundaries
- [ ] refactor codebase to use appropriate types where needed

title: Better Mono repo setup
body:
Right now we are having mono repo (have multiple packages in same repo) but still not really as the dependencies are not as per the repo.
We should start looking into managing bit better using mono repo tools like `lerna`, `yarn workspaces` and similar and of course making sure the development setup is not impacted at the same time.
Lot of existing OSS follows the similar setup like `react`, `create-react-app` as well. This way it will be easier to visualise the deps which are used for that package, making it more manageable and also reduce friction for more packages as we release

title: Support extensions in Excalidraw
body:
This is a space to discuss developing an `@excalidraw/extensions` package.  Per correspondence with @dwelle, "extensions" would be reviewed by @excalidraw, while "plugins" would be community-made without review by @excalidraw.  (Both would be decoupled from `@excalidraw/excalidraw`.)

Possible roadmap:
- Merge `ExcalidrawElement` subtype functionality into `@excalidraw/excalidraw`.
- Decide how to connect `@excalidraw/extensions` to [excalidraw.com](https://excalidraw.com) subdomains for optional usage of MathJax and whatever else the community might contribute (like UML, custom fonts, etc).
- Ship `@excalidraw/extensions` with MathJax as an initial extension illustrating the system.

cc @dwelle @ad1992 @baptiste

title: Undo selecting last shape after freedraw breaks the workflow
body:
What I'm trying to do:

1. Make a shape
2. Freedraw 
3. Realize I've freedrawn with the wrong color (oops) so press Cmd+Z to erase freedraw
4. Change the stroke color
5. Freedraw again (now with the right color)

Actual sequence:

1. Make a shape
2. Freedraw 
3. Realize I've freedrawn with the wrong color (oops) so press Cmd+Z to erase freedraw
4. **Excalidraw selects the last shape**
5. Change the stroke color
6. **Alas, this changed the selected shape's color instead**
7. **Realize the mistake and press on the canvas to try to make the color selection popup disappear**
8. **This causes a dot on the canvas (because freedraw is still selected)**
9. **Cmd+Z to get rid of the dot**
10. **Cmd+Z to undo the change of the shape color**
11. **Select pointer tool**
12. **Press on the canvas with the pointer to get rid of all selection**
13. **Select freedraw tool**
14. Change the stroke color
15. Freedraw again (now with the right color)

These steps in the middle seem unfortunate but I do them many times a day.

I'm not sure what the right fix is (which is why I've described the sequence). I think I'd expect that Cmd+Z'ing the freedraw doesn't select the last shape.

author: albertobissacco

title: [FR] Identical Drag/Click Behavior (straight lines)
body:
# Match Behavior Across Drag and By-Click Modes of Drawing

I am a by-click drawer, meaning when I want to place a graphical object (be it line, circle, et cetera), I click once at the starting point (starting vertex, circle center), then again at drawing end. Excalidraw tries to offer an optimized multi-function approach to this: whereas most tools are drag-only, one can drag or by-click place lines and arrows, the former creating single-leg elements with the latter allowing for multi-leg. However, this precludes by-clickers from placing angle-clamped lines, for example, or placing other features like circles by-click. It would be awesome if, where the two aren't specifically unique (i.e. for quicker drawing of single-leg lines), the behavior was identical between drag and by-click methods of drawing. AFAIK, this includes modifying/adding the following features:

1. By-Click Rectangles+Diamonds+Circles (click first for bottom-left vertex/center/center respectively, second for top-right/top-right/center)
    - Essentially, flip Alt and non-Alt behavior
2. Shift-Clamping of By-Click Arrows+Lines
3. Multi-Leg Shift-Clamp Support
    - Probably makes sense to lump into this FR for the sake of compatibility with by-clicks multi-leg behavior.

These features would really help CADdies like me :)

## Similar Issues

This FR is in the spirit of #5255, and my own frustrations trying to draw angle-clamped lines by-click.

author: 7flash

title: feat: Show connection status in the UI if the websocket connection fails
body:
I have noticed that there are no status indicators in the GUI to indicate when the websocket connection to the collab server has an error.  This is confusing sometimes.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Open excalidraw.com
2. Start collaboration
3. Open the link in another tab/browser
4. Disconnect your wifi

**Expectation:** I would expect to see some indication somewhere in the UI that the websocket connection has been dropped and that the client is attempting to reconnect.

**Current behavior:**  The green 'collaboration' indicator still stays lit, and the number of connected peers remains the same, while errors get printed in the console each time socket.io tries to reconnect:

```
Firefox can’t establish a connection to the server at wss://oss-collab-us2.excalidraw.com/socket.io/?EIO=3&transport=websocket. [56.74fb6229.chunk.js:2:75312](https://excalidraw.com/static/js/56.74fb6229.chunk.js)
The connection to wss://oss-collab-us2.excalidraw.com/socket.io/?EIO=3&transport=websocket was interrupted while the page was loading.
```

**Consequence:** It looks like you are still connected to the server, even when you aren't.

**Possible fix:**
Here is how we could track the connection status.
In `Collab.tsx`, the socket.io client is registered.  See https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/excalidraw-app/collab/Collab.tsx#L419
We could add listeners there for `connect_error`, `connect` and `disconnect`, according to the documentation here: https://socket.io/docs/v3/client-socket-instance/#events
Those listeners could perhaps call `this.excalidrawAPI.updateScene({ wsConnectionState: 'connected' | 'disconnected' | 'error' })` to inform the App about the connection state, storing it in a new field inside of `AppState`.

I haven't made a GUI mockup, but the `CollabButton` in `LayerUI` could maybe be re-styled to look different when the connection is dropped and the attempt to reconnect is in progress.  Either that and/or a discrete text message (e.g. "You are offline.  Attempting to re-connect...") could be shown in the corner next to the collab button.  I would be open for ideas here.

Would this be something that you would consider adding to the core?  I will probably develop it in any event for my fork, and I would be happy to create a pull request if you're interested :)

title: Deleted elements preventing persistence
body: Hi all, we've forked the Excalidraw client over at https://github.com/peoplerenaissance/excalidraw and have been loving it so far. Changes in our fork include swapping the backend from Firebase to an HTTP REST API that supports the same persistence operations (among other UI adjustments for our product's use case), and we've had many successful collaborative drawing sessions so far. Unfortunately, today we discovered a drawing that won't automatically save, which I believe is related to https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5108, filtering deleted elements prior to persisting: For our use case, I'll make a temporary [fix in our fork](https://github.com/peoplerenaissance/excalidraw/commit/746b55362bf620a5e7041399f64169d0500be94a#diff-d57c40d527dc56c4dba8b89685a3b2e2e90c3a09094693a7eaf068ad1d2d76ea) to sync all elements, including deleted ones, but I'd love some pointers into understanding how this might be caused and if we can prevent it in the future, or if we can help contribute to an upstream fix. Thanks!

author: theabhayprajapati

title: improve shape<->cursor alignment for proportional resize / angle clamping
body:
We should improve how we fit/align lines/shapes to the cursor position when creating shapes while holding shift to maintain aspect ratio, or clamp angles.

In the examples below, we want to behave in a way that Figma does (but it applies to most other apps out there).

### rectangles (squares)

Here's excalidraw when creating a square by holding a shift:

![excal_proportional_resize_square](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/181919239-6a1e57cc-060d-47fd-88ea-35f6bc09752f.gif)

Here's figma (notice that the cursor always aligns perfectly to the bounding box):

![figma_proportional_resize_square](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/181919243-649d1a17-65e0-4f51-86e6-64f85a9aa632.gif)

But, it works correctly even in Excalidraw when the square (or a rectangle) already exists, and you resize it:

![excal_proportional_resize_square_existing](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/181919456-6a96b182-38e4-424d-84f2-e7b3c25f6ee3.gif)

### lines/arrows

Excalidraw line angle clamping by holding shift:

![excal_line_angle_clamping](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/181919357-e7f84b5d-920b-43d0-9b0b-5555af60d13a.gif)

Figma (notice that the cursor is always best-effort aligned, where you can draw a [normal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Normal_(geometry)) from the line's tip to the cursor):

![figma_line_angle_clamping](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/181919394-7c6c7439-8aaf-46e3-8964-dbb805a982f6.gif)

So we want the lines to be aligned like this (in black are actual lines/cursor position, in red is the ideal):

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/181919795-48eb510d-e211-464f-a208-b8b22ba4b2c7.png)

title: tab-sync can be triggered before initial init
body:
Not sure whether to consider this a bug, but the tab syncing handler can get triggered before the initial scene init is done, which could result in bugs down the line. It certainly wasn't build with that in mind way so I _would_ consider it a bug.

Steps:

1. open a shareable link which prompts for confirmation
2. upon confirming the modal, the `visibilitychange` is triggered, which syncs state and updates the scene, before the initial scene initialization is finished

title: Scenes not being auto-saved...
body:
On multiple occasions (at least 6-8) I have lost all my work when navigating within Excalidraw to another scene to reference some information. **The last saved status, shows on my active scene (where the work is lost) to have been saved minutes to hours ago.** This is resulting in a significant amount of work and detail (sometimes up to an hours worth) being lost, and needing to be manually recreated. I have on occasion had to redo designs up to 3 times. This is unacceptable. Nor should I need to remember to check the "last saved" time on the scene every-time I navigate a way.

- Is there a way to FORCE a save to the cloud?
- Is there a way to present an alert on the page/in the scene that clearly indicates that nothing has been auto-saved for n-minutes?
- Optimally this will be fixed, but the above two features would help mitigate as well.

The only workaround, is to save the scene locally and reload it. This requires remembering to check "last saved" and then manually save the .excalidraw file. This is a FANTASTIC application, but the stability of saving and continually loss of work is making it unreliable.

- Yes, confirming the network is available and connected to the internet.

author: vjeux

title: panning on trackpad on chrome tends to trigger history bf behavior
body:
Panning canvas (two fingers) on touchpad works fine (no back/forward history behavior triggered), until you hover over a non-canvas DOM element (which we likely don't prevent in various ways we do with canvas), which triggers browser bf behavior. That's fine.

But in chrome (at least on MacOS), if you cancel the bf change (or it doesn't leave the app domain and you go back to canvas, e.g. in case of Excalidraw+), even panning the canvas tends to trigger the bf behavior.

- [ ] does it reproduce on Windows laptop trackpad?

author: pomdtr

title: Downgraded from React 18 back to 17 due to stability issues and problems with .updateScene calls.
body:
Coinciding with the upgrade of Obsidian-Excalidraw to React 18 and migrating to `ReactDOM.createRoot` for creating the Excalidraw component I've been experiencing various random issues. Two in particular
1. Excalidraw and/or Obsidian screen going blank on certain operations. One example is this bug: [#738](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/738).
2. Executing multiple `updateScene()` calls within a function results in updateScene calls to be dropped. I can trace back the point in time when it stopped working to the commit when I moved to `.createRoot`. [#747](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/747)

I have found that if I space `.updateScene()` calls by placing them in separate `window.requestAnimationFrame()` calls the `updateScene()` issue has gone away, however, the stability issue remained... or rather it could be that the stability issue is caused by `requestAnimationFrame()`, however without that I wasn't able to overcome the issue mentioned in bullet 2.

For now, it seems the downgrade has resolved immediate problems, however, I'd like to understand if I am misusing the package in some way that is leading to the above issues, or if something needs to be changed in the Excalidraw package.

author: ryan-di

title: Disconnected users don't disappear from the board in collab mode
body:
Hello! Thanks for the awesome project.

I've noticed some issues when users leave the collaborative mode.
They continue to be in the list of connected users and their cursors do not disappear until a new user connects, after which the state is updated.
![excalidraw-collab-bug-1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6034438/186505451-7acf1e19-0818-4b7b-b781-6823e45d84f8.png)

Also, sometimes the counter of clients does not match the number of their avatars
![excalidraw-bug-2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6034438/186505473-54b2bbfd-e728-4974-bf04-4497c1cb8ab9.png)

Both screenshots were taken after all users disconnected about half an hour ago.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Start collaboration mode.
2. Join a board from multiple tabs.
3. Close all tabs opened in the previous step.
4. Go to the tab with the collaboration organizer and wait a bit, the users are still displayed as active.
5. Open a new tab and join the board, this step will update the state of the clients.

### Expected behavior
After disconnecting a client, its cursor and avatar should be removed, and the client counter should be updated.

### System
macOS 12.5.1 (arm64)
Chrome 104.0.5112.101
author: nikitapozdeev

title: Theme settings are reset after turning a collaboration session on and off
body:
Hi!

I ran into some problem. I chose the "dark" theme, but sometimes after turning the collaboration session on and off, the theme resets to "light".

### Steps to reproduce
1. Clear localStorage and refresh the page.
2. Choose "dark" theme. In localStorage theme also switches to "dark".
3. Start collab session.
4. Refresh the page. Here the theme automatically switches to "light" and that's strange. But in localStorage the theme is still "dark".
5. Stop collab session. After this, the theme in localStorage sets to "light" as well.

### Expected behavior
Theme settings should not be reset after turning a collaboration session on and off.

### System
macOS 12.5.1 (arm64)
Chrome 104.0.5112.101

author: anatoliibakhtin

title: Partial Selection Mode
body:
In some design programs it is possible to change the mode of the selection tool to make selections faster and easier.
There are usually 2 modes, full selection of the object (the current mode in excalidraw) and partial selection.

The partial selection allows that if an area of ​​the object is selected, the object is activated as selected without the need to select the object completely.

I think an option could be added in the configuration to be able to choose the mode.

EXAMPLES:
In example A it can be seen that with a minimum selection the two boxes could be selected.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7627504/187867275-b91f641d-47f1-4174-8801-8c79a54a4a7c.png)

In example B it is necessary to make a larger selection (more time) and it is also necessary to manually remove the small box.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7627504/187867311-f9c3b07f-713a-4943-a4de-65fc3299daa5.png)
author: javiavid

title: Support for arcs
body:
Hello, I love excalidraw, but I am so frustrated by how I can't just create an arc. I would love for me to be able to create 'partial circles', and I personally think the best way to implement them for the end user is like how inkscpae does it. Double click on it to enter edit mode, like what happens when you double click lines, and there is a little dot on the top of a circle that can be dragged. If its grabbed and dragged, the circle becomes a partial circle, becoming an arc, with the things being removed around the center of the circle:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48590492/188109191-e75f2ed9-f7d9-491c-9e4b-c0d6084a9aff.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48590492/188109113-57f3e4da-ad93-4f23-9f53-0f81d97da663.png)

author: josephp27

title: Double tap + hold on mobile make the shape behave weirdly
body:
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13915148/189313631-e777c742-cfa3-44d8-a7b5-247e50c7b849.mp4

**Browser**: Android Google Chrome v 105.0

**Reproduction steps**: 

1. Launch excalidraw on mobile 
2. Create a shape 
3. Move it anywhere
4. Double tap + hold the second tap
5. You are in text insertion mode + you are moving the shape 

**Expected behaviour**: 

1. Double tap + hold the second tap
2. You are in text insertion mode but you can't move the shape

On a side note: It isn't reproducible on desktop. So I believe it has to do with the "touchStart" and "touchEnd". :thinking: 

From what I have seen in the code and the correlation between the bug and the reproduction step, my gut feeling would be that the first tap is recorded as "tap start" AND "tape end" but the second is only recorded as "tap start" but doesn't end because we hold it. 

Although the reproduction step seems quite weird to do, I would argue that sometimes you will eventually do it by accident because tactile surfaces behave weirdly. :sweat_smile:

title: When editing linear element and selecting a tool on the toolbar it jumps back to the selection tool
body:
@ad1992 - I think this is one for you. I've been at it for 2 hours now, but I can't (yet) figure out the root cause. The issue does not affect excalidraw.com, but can be reproduced in the Visual Studio component and in Obsidian as well.

This issue only happens when `appState.editingLinearElement !== null` before clicking a tool icon on the toolbar.

Effectively what happens is the tool the user clicked is selected for a moment and then a next setState automatically sets the "selection" tool. 

I was suspecting collaboration mode since this is missing from both the VSCode implementation and from Obsidian... but this proved to be a dead end.

My other assumption is this could be related to the same issue as in #5508. It seems as if the old state is passed as appState in this call:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/9cccac1458c8ff6b79452305cbbed7032522f18b/src/components/App.tsx#L1150-L1158

Visual Studio Code (FYI: @pomdtr):

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/190693697-d802cffa-b143-4779-b727-98a88c44af9d.mp4

Obsidian:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/190694164-1e813af1-28a4-4d03-980e-282275e855fb.mp4

title: "Backlink" á la Obsidian/Logseq disappears after coming back to it
body:
Hi everybody,

today I tried to implement backlinks to sort of connect multiple excalidraw boards. The way I did it was by simply writing and adding a link to a textblock on (let's call it) **"board 1"**. This link is essentially an exported excalidraw board - let's call that board **"board 2"**. Now after clicking on the link, it takes me to the destined **board 2**, great! I do the same now on board 2, in order to reference it to **board 1**. Whilst the **link on board 2 will bring me back to board 1**, the _link on board 1 has vanished_. On opening _board 2_ seperately, I see that the _backlink is also missing_. 

So I end up with no links :(

Have you guys tried this? Is there a solution or workaround to it? I would really like to use it that way, since it is a workflow which really helps.

title: Double-tap to create a new text item closes the on-screen keyboard
body:
If you are editing a text item and double tap to create a new one, it will close the on-screen keyboard on Android. This is not a blocker but it's slows down the flow of adding multiple text items. As every time you want to add a new one you have to first un-select the previous one.

Steps to reproduce the behavior (see recording below):
- Edit or add a text item.
- While the item is still in edit mode (blinking cursor), double tap somewhere else to create a new one.
- Observe how a new text item becomes editable, but the keyboard closes.

The recording from Obsidian.md but I can easily reproduce it in Google Chrome. I'm running Android 12 on a Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+. Google Chrome version 105.0.5195.136, this has been happening for months.

Note: Originally reported in  [zsviczian / obsidian-excalidraw-plugin #823](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/823).

![keyboard-closes](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/116038/192142930-dbf5de30-3cd6-410b-b561-782e0cac6ebe.gif)
author: manuelmorales

title: Text jumps when double-tapped to edit on Android
body:
Sometimes text items will jump when double-tapped to edit them. See video attached. I'm using a Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+ with the on-screen floating Samsung keyboard.

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Double tap to add a text item.
2. Tap outside to un-select it.
3. Double tap the item again to edit it.
4. Observe how the text moves top a different place.

Seems to happen randomly.  Please repeat the edit-unselect process repeatedly if it doesn't trigger the first time.

The recording from Obsidian.md but I can easily reproduce it in Google Chrome. I'm running Android 12 on a Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+. Google Chrome version 105.0.5195.136, this has been happening for months.

Note: Originally reported in  [zsviczian / obsidian-excalidraw-plugin #822](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/822).

![jumping-text](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/116038/192143029-01980d32-0a0a-4494-a17e-b632795e15ae.gif)

title: rethinking the defaults
body:
I think it's a good idea to review whether our default settings and values are the most useful.

I think we should especially review `text align` & `stroke width`.

# generic elements

| setting          | current default | ok/proposal |
|------------------|-----------------|:-----------:|
| stroke color     | black           |      ✅      |
| background color | transparent     |    ✅?       |
| fill             | hachure         |    ✅        |
| stroke width     | thin            |    bold?     |

author: dumbdreamed

title: [Discussion] Excalidraw accessibility
body:
I concur with @leighmcculloch in #5756 about increasing accessibility in color-spaces.  Another approach might be to add [WCAG](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/Understanding_WCAG) checkers.

MathJax provides various accessibility features.  I recently updated #2993 to store the MathJax speech-enriched contents in `ExcalidrawMathElement.customData.ariaLabel`.  But Excalidraw's support for screen readers presently seems limited mainly to the interface, not the canvas contents.

I am opening this issue as a space for discussing possible improvements to Excalidraw accessibility in general, whether related to vision or otherwise.

cc @baptiste @dwelle @ad1992
author: DanielJGeiger

title: Usualibity improvment - change the keybind of elipse?
body:
Hey! I'll take a a shot in the dark and don't really expect an answer.

I was suprised that the ellipse key have moved from E to O and I feel like it have made the user experience worse.
I think one of the strength of excalidraw was having common shortcuts mapped to accessible keys allowing for quick prototyping. 
And the simple learning curve for beginners.

Previosly when eclipse was bounded to E: 
+ Hand doesn't need any movement as it can rest on the left homerow - as most common shapes are within range. 
+ Low overhead, purely focusing on drawing as you don't need to hunt and peck for the O key. 
+ Better learning curve for beginners as the rule for the first letter to the shape makes sense. 


 Using the key O for elipse:
  +  Uninutive, all other short cuts to shape corrosponds to the  first letter of the shape, e (**a**rrow, **d**iamond, **l**ine) and not by the look of the shape (O).
  + You have to move the hand across the otherside of the keyboard to draw "common" shape. In that time I could draw 1-2 elipses before reaching the O the key. Severly slowing down the drawing process.
  + Touch typist need to look down at the keyboard as moving the left hand to the right side is very unnatural and error-prone because of the relavtively big jump. It sometimes forces the users to look at the keyboard.
  +  ^above distracts you from the drawing task and context switches you out from it, losing focus on whats important: Drawing! 
  + CTRL+Z supports the idea of "one hand no movement" and the DEL key should be inutive instead of using the operation erase
  + (Positive) migrating other users from other prototyping platforms 
  + (Positive) Using the "4" key is an alternative.... but not as great!

author: solomonshalom

title: Copying selection as PNG results in a flicker on all open windows
body:
Hi folks, 

Upfront, thanks to the entire team for all the effort that is poured into making this tool. It has made everything easier and was partially responsible for saving our team a lot of troubles when we were forced to go remote during the pandemic. 

---

I'm here today because I seem to be facing a lot of issue in one regard. Before I get there, here is my workflow: 

1. Design something on excalidraw 
2. Select said design 
3. Copy to clipboard
4. Add to notion / send to engineer (via slack, teams, etc) 

I keep everything previously designed as well. So I have one HUGE canvas. 

The issue right now seems to be coming from step (3) in the workflow. Whenever I copy to clipboard as a PNG, all the open windows seem to be getting focused and losing focus. 

This is best illustrated with a gif: 

![4VPe5ANWbd](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/240368/196894030-cfd6f93b-1eaf-45cd-a163-2fd72dd74a54.gif)

If you notice above, the instant I hit on the "copy to clipboard as PNG" button, both the windows start flickering. Once this happens, I can't type in any window or do any interaction because the focus just keeps going off (I attempted to write `notepad` in the run window by the side and only ended up registering `n` there)

I am able to mitigate this in 2 ways: 

1. Restarting the machine (which is my last resort, since I might have a lot of work being done then) 
2. Locking the machine. Doing so, and leaving it locked for a few seconds and then relogging in seems to fix this problem. 

---

I hope that I was able to convey the point across. Please do feel free to ask for more clarification as required. 

Thanks again for your efforts! Cheers ✨

title: Pasting multi-line as individual text elements
body:
I'm often in a situation where I want to paste many lines that I would like to treat as individual text element but right now it pastes them as one text element.

Use cases:
* When doing re-orgs, I want to paste a bunch of names collected from somewhere else.
* I'm doing a minecraft challenge to try to get every single item of the game. I want to paste all the remaining items and group them to figure out what are the things I have remaining to do.

Proposal:
* If you paste multiline elements without having anything selected, it creates one text element per line. [New Behavior]
* If you double click to create a text element and paste, it pastes it as a single text element. [Already existing behavior]
author: vjeux

title: Bug - Image horizontal/vertical flip action broken
body:
Hi folks, I just saw this issue in production and made curious enough to clone this repo.

### Problem

The flip action on images is not working. The vertical flip seems to be working, but it's incorrect since it can't perform the horizontal flip it needs.
![excalidraw_flip_image_bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/40394991/197348685-1572fa32-1869-4d42-8d0f-3ae7f1fafab9.gif)


### Cause

After #5752 the flipping coordinate calculations have changed.
This introduced an edge-case for Image elements, which rely on the scaling on the x axis to perform a flip.
The way of calculating the new scaling value, is to check the initial size and the future size of the element.
However, for a flip, these two sizes are the same, and the from NW direction the scale X will always be 0.

### Solution
Currently, the 0 scale exception is handled by defaulting to the starting scale, which is always => don't scale at all.
A possible solution would be to default the 0 scale to -1, at least for image elements.

In case the solution is fine by you, I can prepare a PR.
Cheers!

author: xuanswe

title: Proposal: find shapes in personal and imported libraries should be easier
body:
Assume each user has 100 shapes in personal library (not count yet imported shapes in Excalidraw library). There are 2 problems when working with this shapes.

1. Currently the shapes in libraries are ordered by insertion. It would be nice if users can drag/drop to rearrange these shapes to logical positions.
2. The sidebar, which contains shapes in libraries should have a search text box. Users could enter a text to filter the shapes. To be able to that, users are allowed to add name and keywords to each shape in personal library.
author: xuanswe

title: Allow binding text to shape by double clicking anywhere in the shape
body:
Right now we allow binding the text to a shape by pressing "Enter" or by clicking on the approx center of the shape.  
If there is some text already bound to the shape then we allow editing the text by clicking anywhere on the shape.

I think we should also allow double-clicking on the shape to bind the text to the shape as that's one the way users would expect to add text when binding.
 Checked a few platforms -  Figjam, tldraw, Tryeraser, figjam, and whimsical allow the same as well.

This would also improve the visibility of this feature as well.

title: Choose the container selected by user when multiple container intersecting
body:
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11256141/201027960-c5062a8f-c52e-4d66-a44a-8101b733052f.mp4

Right now we try to compute which container to choose based on the pointer coords and choose the one with the highest z-index. This often leads to choosing the wrong container especially when containers are intersecting. 
Instead, we should always give preference to the container which the user actually selects and that's more intuitive as well.
Miro, whimsical, eraser works the similar way as well irrespective of containers are transparent or not.

author: patrik-csak

title: BUG: color picker is misaligned when parent container has the contain:strict rule applied
body:
The editor redesign (#5780) introduced the use of `position: fixed` layout elements. As a result positioning the color picker based on the BoundingClientRect of the pickerButton results in an offset, because Excalidraw in Obsidian is hosted in a container that has `contain: strict` setting.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/201535938-300846d7-b8c1-484e-b064-e2b9a0e53dda.png)

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/640affe7c06fc20cf93787b67cc8dea7aa50c51b/src/components/ColorPicker.tsx#L368-L397

The contain strict rule does two things:
1. It tells the browser engine that the contents inside this view can never go outside. This is a performance critical rule because otherwise in a multi pane configuration (like Obsidian), the engine will think that any change in any pane can affect other panes, causing layout algorithm to basically recompute the entire app instead of just that specific view (for example, typing in an editor would possibly lag more).
2. It causes the layout algorithm to forcefully set the size of the view based on the workspace layout, and never be affected by whatever is inside the view. Without this, it is possible for some combination of DOM and CSS inside the view to cause the view to overflow in such a way that it grows the container, which would completely wreck the workspace layout.

@barnabasmolnar, please reconsider those editor layout changes that involve `position:fixed`, to improve the deployability of the excalidraw package.

title: Do not bind to container when using text tool
body: Don't bind to the container when using the text tool as no container is selected so it could be ambiguous which container is selected especially when containers intersect as mentioned in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5860 Checked a few other tools like Miro, whimsical, and Eraser work in a similar way. author: DanielJGeiger

title: Feature: Post-It sections/frames, which are own Excalidraws again
body:
There is one feature on miro.com (which is a good tool for visual graphs/presentation/process planning), which is pretty helpful. You can have a big map, with many subsections, which are contained in a frame like a picture or selectable on their own. I would call it post-it like frames, because they feel like you just work on many different things on one big visual board, where you zoom in the part, which interests you most.

I think this would be super helpful, if Excalidraw has a similar workflow. Even if you just add a post-it background or frame, which we can freely draw. So it's mainly a visual expansion. But if you try it out and might feel, you should make them full subsections, which are self-contained, it might would be a great idea.

See it as brain strom suggestion. I don't like to use miro.com if Excalidraw can do the same! 👍 Thanks for making the best plugin in Obsidian!

author: danielbeutner

title: rewrite contextMenu to use appState
body:
In https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5893 we've moved from `React.render` (v17) to `createRoot` (v18), but that broke the editor for apps that use react v17 (which we still list as compatible).

For now, we shouldn't be breaking React 17 apps, so let's instead rewrite the contextMenu to use `appState` and render as part of the normal render lifecycle.

What we need to do:

- instead of calling [`ContextMenu.push()`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/1f117995d90a1bb6c452791eff62c3efad66ee61/src/components/ContextMenu.tsx#L115-L144) (see https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/1f117995d90a1bb6c452791eff62c3efad66ee61/src/components/App.tsx#L5993-L6001), set `this.setState({ contextMenu: { options, top, left } })`. So, we'll need to:
- Add an appropriate type to [`AppState`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/1f117995d90a1bb6c452791eff62c3efad66ee61/src/types.ts#L93). Only the `options`, `top`, `left` needs to be defined. The other props the `.push()` method currently takes can be read from state/App instance directly.
- render the `ContextMenu` component directly to [`<div className="excalidraw-contextMenuContainer" />`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/1f117995d90a1bb6c452791eff62c3efad66ee61/src/components/App.tsx#L592)
author: dwelle

title: Line midpoint often incorrect

author: dwelle

title: Redesigning the color picker
body:
Playground: https://codesandbox.io/s/cocky-cohen-f8lris?file=/src/App.js

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204290680-848da27c-0081-4b3f-84f0-8a8df16256a0.png)

The basic principle:

![excal-color-picker-redesign-poc](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204362627-90a1af8c-054a-4af9-b186-d4827b4e7a07.gif)

### The idea

1. To address the issue of having too small choice of colors (to cover most use cases), we'll introduce a shade color picker. A row of ~5 shades for each base color.

2. Revamp the default colors. Either we just change shades, or we use a different color swatch altogether (we are currently using https://yeun.github.io/open-color/).

3. Use the same base colors for both the stroke and background.

    First off, both should support _white_, _black_, and _transparent_ colors. Also, you should be able to use the same color for both without copy/pasting the HEX value.

4. Probably still use a different shade for stroke and background.

    While we will use the same base colors, the default shade for background will be ~one shade lighter than the stroke. Stroke needs more contrast, while background needs to be less strong.
    
    Also, the common case is that you create a container with a background, and want to pick a matching stroke color which is still distinguishable from the background.

5. Show your most used colors even outside the color popup so you don't have to open it every time.

    Below, (1) is your "quick palette". Click to set color. (2) shows active color. If you want more options, you click on (2) to open the color popup.

    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204294668-70baec30-7756-4e18-b2b7-220866b739d1.png)

### FAQ

- **question:** Why a preset of shades instead of a full-blown color picker (wheel) allowing you to pick any color?
    - **answer:** Excalidraw's principle is simplicity and low friction. We want to give you a curated set of tools to create diagrams quickly without having to obsess about details.

        As such, we want to pick a set of colors that all look good by default, so you don't have to spend time on picking them yourselves.
        
        On top of that, a limited selection promotes consistency.

        That said, we still leave the option to add a custom color picker open.

- **question:** Why just 5 shades?
    - **answer:** While we could add more shades, such as 10 per color, it would again create too much choice (13 colors x 10 shades). If we pick our 5 shades well it should cover most use cases.

- **question:** What about keyboard shortcuts?
    - **answer:** Keyboard shortcuts will still be a thing! Sadly, we'll have to break them 🙁 (for those who have them in their muscle memory). Refer below:
    
        ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204296254-1d9c4b9f-be35-4bb1-a20a-bb0064183003.png)


### Open questions

1. Will it be obvious (enough) how to open the color picker? The first five colors sets the active color, the 6th (active) color opens the color picker. How to make it more obvious?

2. Once you pick a shade, should the shade become the default for new colors as well (until you change it again)? I think it should.

3. What base colors to pick, and what shades? Especially the lightest and darkest shades.

5. Allow to customize "top picks"? Currently (on excalidraw.com), it uses the last 5 colors used on canvas that are not the default colors.

----

fix #4842
should take into account: #3643 #5872 #4086
WONTFIX for now: #5582 (full blown color pickers/wheel)

author: dwelle

title: proposal: deploy from a `release` branch
body:
Proposal to create a `release` branch and auto-release from there. We'd keep working from and merging to `master`. Once we want to make a release, we'd merge `master` into `release`.

Reasoning:

1) Each release we force-reload clients. If we make 10 releases a day, that's potentially 10 reloads for people. Each reload can be pretty annoying.
2) Each release invalidates SW cache (haven't checked, but hopefully just for the changed build artifacts. But, it would pretty much contain at least the main bundle anyway). Meaning, unnecessary increase in bandwidth for users, and for us.
3) The two reasons above makes us less inclined to make frequent releases. If we want to split a feature into several PRs, we can't (or it's hard to do so). If we want to make several related or stacked PRs, same issue.
4) Same goes for npm releases. If we want to do larger API rewrites, we'd likely need to disable the release action for the duration.
5) Similarly, some heavy CI actions such as deploying to Docker is unnecessarily blocking / slowing down the queue.

/cc @ad1992

author: tmielsch

title: Show a message about potential reason for filesystem permission error
body:
When permission error is detected on filesystem save/open, we sohuld display a modal with steps people can do to check whether their browser filesystem access is disabled.

The error on Chrome looks like:

```
DOMException: The request is not allowed by the user agent or the platform in the current context.
```

The steps to check on Chrome are these: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5773#issuecomment-1299947849

We should check whether other browsers have similar mechanisms and what their error messages look like.

And we should also mention what it means if the filesystem setting is grayed out, so they know it's likely due to their system administrator (e.g. the company).

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5811
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5773

title: pan in edit mode changes zoom
body:
1. in edit mode
2. zoom in/out
3. immediately after
4. hold space key + use mouse to pan
5. zoom level is changed

After playing around with this, I understood that the when releasing space, the last button clicked is clicked again. so if I press anywhere else in the doc (step 3) it avoids the bug. also, when this happens you can clearly see that the zoom button regains focus (blue border), although clicking it did not *appear* to keep the focus. 

I know this is a small thing but I'm reporting this as a bug since I think this is a very common workflow. I do this all the time when I land on a new excalidraw I immediately orient myself by zoom then pan.
author: itaysk

title: [FEATURE] Add option to move shape into existing group/create shape inside the group
body:
Excalidraw already puts new text nodes into the deepest group located at point of text insertion. This behaviour seems to be unique to text nodes; any other shape is created free and there doesnt seems to be any option to put them automatically inside a group. So if a need for this arises, the user is forced to do these things:
1. Ungroup objects until the target group is destructured, which can be nested somewhat deeply(usually the case if you use excalidraw say for modelling a UI with nested components)
2. put a new shape into place
3. recreate groups that were destroyed in step 1 in reverse order.

This is prone to mistakes, most commonly, dragging objects that are not supposed to be moved. The undo can't always be relied upon, from my experience(especially in multiuser scenario).

So what can be done?
There might be several ways of addressing this issue:
- add a context menu items "move selected object into the group..." and list the groups that are currently under the cursor.
- add an option to put new shape inside existing group, activated by holding down a key perhaps
- Implement object tree view, where you can move shapes between groups with a simple drag-and-drop. Something like this
![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4126782/207320118-d2ace115-0438-4f89-8e79-7d772c0b88c8.png)

author: sedghi

title: HamburgerMenu public API
body:
As per discussion in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5960, we want to expose public API for customizing HamburgerMenu.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/208890602-c01daf9d-5598-4934-81d8-b939b04d1f22.png)

There are 2 main ideas:

1. Always render default items, and additionally allow to add custom ones.
    
    **Pros:**
    
    - Updates to the app (adding/changing default items) will always be reflected to host apps
    
    **Cons:**
    
    - Hard to allow adding items in between default ones
    - Hard to allow to remove default items you don't want

3. Render the default hamburger menu only if the host app doesn't render by itsels.

    **Pros:**
    
    - Easy to customize everything
    
    **Cons:**
    
    - Updates to the app (adding/changing default items) will not be reflected to host apps — they'll have to track the changes and update the menu by themselves.

title: typescript backwards compatibility
body:
Would love to use the [`satisfies`](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/release-notes/typescript-4-9.html#the-satisfies-operator) operator, but it'll break types for host apps running on TS < 4.9.

Post 1.0 we should be way more mindful of breaking like this, but right now, IMO I'd do it.

For most projects, upgrading to 4.9 should require none to very few changes to the codebase.
