title: Locking text does not work with convertToExcalidrawElements
body:
## Expected result
When creating text container from `ExcalidrawElementSkeleton` with `convertToExcalidrawElements` if label is explicitly locked from editing it should not be editable on the canvas.

## Actual result
Text can be edited.

## Minimal reproduction code:
```tsx
const Wrapper = () => {
  const elements = convertToExcalidrawElements({
      x: 300,
      y: 300,
      width: 300,
      height: 300,
      backgroundColor: '#ffec99',
      fillStyle: 'solid',
      strokeWidth: 1,
      strokeStyle: 'solid',
      strokeColor: 'transparent',
      roughness: 0,
      type: 'rectangle',
      label: {
        text: 'Card content',
        fontFamily: FONT_FAMILY.Helvetica,
        locked: true, // LABEL IS EXPLICITLY LOCKED
        strokeColor: '#1e1e1e',
        groupIds: ['the id'],
      },
      groupIds: ['the id']
  })
  return (
    <Excalidraw initialData={{ elements }}/>
  )
}
```

While the mindmap node is selected. (it will just be like another icon such as text or shape)

User can
- Tab - to create a child

- Del to delete the node
- Double click to edit the node

* in hierarchy (or, text in TOC like fashion)

The benefit of adding this is that the brainstorming will be a lot faster without needing to juggle with placements, tabs, etc.
and thus, organizing text will be a lot cleaner and faster.

title: BUG: svg export returns pixelated images for SVG inserts
body:
**To reproduce the Problem on excalidraw.com :**

1. Create a new drawing
2. Draw something using the pen tool
3. Right-click Copy as SVG
4. Paste into drawing
5. Export image as svg

**Problem:** The copy-pasted part of the image is inserted in the SVG as a pixelated (png?) image of bad quality.

**expected behaviour:** A copy/pasted SVG inside an excalidraw file should be inserted as vector/svg image in the exported file. 

**side-effect of the bug**: The excalidraw-obsidian plugin uses SVGs to nicely display latex equations inside excalidraw. However with this new behaviour all exports containing math equations look pixelated.

**Note:** This seems to be a BUG that was introduced recently because I was able to export latex equations without any problems just a few weeks ago.

author: sksusha8853

title: Opening a `.excalidraw` file clobbers all ad hoc drawings that weren't saved to a file
body:
# Problem

If a user has excalidraw open with ad hoc drawings (not saved to disk, only stored in browser local storage) and opens an `excalidraw` file via a file explorer a new instance of excalidraw will be opened (if installed via a PWA) and will clobber all stored ad hoc drawings without warning the user.

# Expected Behavior

Excalidraw should warn the user that any ad hoc drawings will be lost if they open a file, and/or allow them to save their ad hoc drawings to disk. If the user had a file from disk open when they attempt to open another file excalidraw should prompt them to save their changes to disk or automatically save the changes to disk for them.

Another option would be to store multiple sessions in browser storage so that all ad hoc drawings don't have to be saved to disk but would be persisted in browser storage.

title: Path to the assets is set incorrectly
body:
When the path to the assets is set by `EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH`, it is used correctly in the editor, but exported with the wrong path. There is one "dist" directory too much.

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/3e334a67ed43d11c6a5a331f82a847c07ff6175d/packages/excalidraw/scene/export.ts#L349

I set the path to the following value:
```
<script>
window.EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH = "http://excaliwp.test/wp-content/plugins/excalidraw/public/assets/";
</script>
```

The editor is looking for the path `http://excaliwp.test/wp-content/plugins/excalidraw/public/assets/excalidraw-assets/Virgil.woff2` but the export contains `http://excaliwp.test/wp-content/plugins/excalidraw/public/assets//dist/excalidraw-assets/Virgil.woff2`

title: Request to Maintain Library Bar Open After Item Selection
body:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/139263307/4f05a57a-45ad-492d-b619-4b6a2ce20bec



Currently, when utilizing the library bar on the right side of the interface, selecting an item automatically closes the library. While a close button is present, its functionality is overridden upon item selection, necessitating users to reopen the library for each item selection.

To enhance usability and streamline workflow, I suggest maintaining the library bar open after item selection. This adjustment would enable users to access multiple items from the library without the need to repeatedly open the library bar.

Retaining the library bar open post-item selection would significantly improve user experience, reducing unnecessary clicks and interruptions to workflow.

Thank you for considering this request to improve the functionality of the library feature.

author: AdamLinCodes

title: Incompatible with next React version
body:
The Excalidraw bundles `react/jsx-runtime`, but not `react` itself. This means that if a new React version changes the interface between jsx and react, it'll break.

You can see this by searching for `__SECRET_INTERNALS_DO_NOT_USE_OR_YOU_WILL_BE_FIRED` in 
https://unpkg.com/@excalidraw/excalidraw@0.17.5/dist/excalidraw.production.min.js
That internal was renamed in https://github.com/facebook/react/commit/f6131653570bbbf62d642ba9343b9cd0ab1ae97c

What should be done here is to configure the Excalidraw build such that `react/jsx-runtime` is not bundled and remain a `require` just like `react` or `react-dom`.

title: `Store` improvements
body:
`Store` was introduced as part of the multiplayer undo / redo #7348, in order to abstract away capturing of local `Change`s to the elements and the (observed) appState. At the time, it was designed to be plugged in front of the existing `History`, without affecting too much the rest of the codebase.

Hence we accepted some tradeoffs, which we should now reconsider, refactor away and improve as part of this initiative.

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7921
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7923
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7930
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7931
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7922
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7925
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7926
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7927
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7928
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7939
```

author: AsiyaBatool

title: Unify `Store` data flow for local updates
body:
Local changes within the editor are still being recorded after the `App` component updates. This means that our data flow of capturing changes is `action` -> `componentDidUpdate` -> `store`, but updates through the `updateScene` API end up in the `Store` before component updates - to filter out yet uncommitted local elements and eliminate potential concurrency issues.

As stated in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/7348#discussion_r1516057935, we should not capture the _increments_ post component update, but emit them from the user actions and apply them to the `Store` directly. Additionally, turning on recording in relevant actions and capturing the changes after component updates does not just seem error-prone, but might also lead to unexpected issues related to `React`'s auto-batching & concurrent mode.

Moreover, from the event sourcing perspective, coupling renders with exactly one user action is just plain wrong - we should be able to have multiple actions as part of one render, as well as one "async" action spread across multiple renders.

> In theory, to support async actions properly, we would likely need to establish transactions on the elements level (with transaction id, rollback possibility, etc.)  in case there are other actions triggered in the meantime - such instances are out of the scope of this initiative.

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Remove scheduling of increments capture and snapshot update post component update
- [ ] Identify and refactor actions which are dependent on multiple `setState` calls or are generally asynchronous
- [ ] Update local store data flow from `action` -> `state` -> `componentDidUpdate` -> `store` into `action` -> `store` -> `state`.
```

> Store should not be considered as a mediator of the state, as it is handling only a subset of state operations. Especially it's interested only in (observed) committed data (no ephemerals).
author: Mrazator

title: Improve handling and exchanging of ephemerals
body:
As their name suggests, _ephemeral_ actions, such as dragging, resizing or drawing, do not fit into the whole `Store` story very well. Ideally, such actions should be streamed over to the other clients as quickly as possible, with as small a footprint as possible. 

However, the biggest weak point currently is that each received remote action updates the `Store` snapshot by deep cloning changed elements. For shared scenes with multiple users simultaneously performing multiple ephemeral actions, it's far from ideal.

The ultimate solution is to avoid deep cloning of elements into the snapshot and not performing "snapshots diffing" at all, which is a far future, as currently we perform the diffing on all local actions. The next best solution, apart from optimising cloning itself, is to keep diffing, but postpone the clone as much as possible. Ideally the clone should be scheduled just once (A) and performed only if it's needed (B). Meaning we could do the following.

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Separate ephemeral updates from committed updates and debounce these updates of snapshot and related necessary cloning per `clientId`. Flush pending updates before locally capturing a change ~ solves (A).
- [ ] Add a logical `source: "local" | "remote"` on the element level (locally computed out of `clientId` or similar), so that we calculate changes only on the "local" updates and clone only remotely modified elements ~ solves (B), also allows to deprecate `filterUncomittedElements`.
- [ ] Consider streaming increments as ephemerals to the other clients and do not perform diffing to obtain them (otherwise it would not be great for perf.). Either way, we will likely want to keep receiving all elements once in a while, to ensure consistency if things go wrong, or at least until increments are SSOT.
- [ ] Consider a different Delta format for ephemerals, containing element ids and expressing operations instead of the state - i.e. imagine dragging thousands of elements, where x and y represent the same Δ on all elements, `Op { x: +10, y: -20 }`.
- [ ] Consider integrating mouse movements into ephemeral `appState`.
```
author: Mrazator

title: Decouple `Store` from the editor
body:
```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [x] Separate `Store` into a standalone package, which could be also potentially used server-side.
- [x] Draw a fine line between the shared functions, utilities & types - likely will need to be separated as well.
```
author: Mrazator

title: Decouple `History` from `Store`
body:
As of now, `History` is controlled by the `StoreAction` and thus coupled to the `Store` component.

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ]  Consider decoupling each other once we hit some, yet unknown, limits.
```
author: Mrazator

title: Expose `Store` emitter to the outside world
body:
After we stabilized the `Store`, especially #7921 and #7923, we might expose the increments emitter to the outside world.

- [ ] Design and expose the API for listening and applying increments - i.e. `onIncrement`, `applyIncrement`, etc.
- [ ] Consider exposing changes also in a JSON patch-compatible format for inter compatibility with other tools
- [ ] Test the `Store` independently, as currently it is being tested indirectly by the `History` integration tests (unit, integration & performance tests)
- [ ] Think about an API for a pluggable Store (far future).
author: Mrazator

title: Migration infrastructure for `Store` increments
body:
Once the schema of the state (or deltas) changes, we should be able to migrate up and down the existing increments as we should treat them as SSOT. As part of this task, prepare migrations strategy and infrastructure for migration of existing increments.
 
```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Prepare migrations strategy and infrastructure for migration of existing increments
- [ ] Syntactic validation of increments (compile time, runtime)
- [ ] Semantic validation of increments (runtime)
```
author: Mrazator

title: Exchange `Store` increments between clients
body:
Exchange the increments as an SSOT between the clients, achieving better granularity when multiple users work on the same element/s (even asynchronously) and eventually sync the changes together, converging to the same state on all clients.

To keep the P2P nature of the collaboration together with E2EE, we will likely need to keep the application of increments & related conflict resolution client-side. However, we could consider opting for stateful coordination between clients, such as holding version vector (VV) remotely, in order to remember which clients are on which versions, optimise communication and define total order by a central authority (CA).

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Deprecate element-based reconciliation based on `version` & `versionNonce`
- [ ] Update the state by applying remote increments based on the API introduced in #7925
- [ ] Define conflict resolution strategy (i.e. LWW) and related order of increments (CA approach above, optionally having logical clocks & version vectors client-side)
- [ ] Define a syncing strategy and related coordination/coordination, such as - stateful CA-based coordination, acknowledging increments server-side, pushing increments to clients directly, rebasing unacknowledged increments client-side, applying increments client-side etc.
- [ ] Consider adding additional metadata to the `Store`, such as VV, to be aware of other remote `Store`s and their versions
- [ ] Consider adding additional metadata to the increments, such as `id` (to ensure they are not applied twice), `sourceId` (to distinguish between local/remote increments), `type` (to distinguish between "committed" and "ephemeral" increments), potentially HLC for client-side order
- [ ] Consider system testing multiple bootstrapped clients simultaneously, similarly to what we do in collab tests, going one level above of integration tests
```
author: Mrazator

title: Persist `Store` increments on the clients
body:
```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Introduce and expose an option to persist increments inside db (i.e. `IndexedDB`) on increment
- [ ] Load persisted increments out of the db on init
- [ ] Keep persisting / loading separated from the domain logic of the store & decoupled from the editor
- [ ] Store snapshot locally and always apply the increments on top of the latest snapshot
- [ ] Consider loading a large log of increments in chunks (due to memory limits)
- [ ] Consider improving perf. of applying changes for a large log of increments
- [ ] Consider sharing the same delta instances between different deltas (flyweight-like for memory)
- [ ] Consider compressing/squashing increments into a more compact form on persist
```
author: Mrazator

title: Load the scene based on the `Store` increments
body: Will be tackled as part of #7928
author: Mrazator

title: Stabilize `Snapshot` diffing
body:
As of now, our `Snapshot` diffing algo is limited by a shallow compare on the level of object properties, meaning it goes just two levels deep. It's due to the instability of reference values inside `appState` and `elements`, as sometimes they mutate or result in new instances even though they didn't change - thus we cannot perform quick reference checks.

For now, it's sufficient for our purposes, but it is not super flexible, not super fast, does not allow deeper nesting and likely our needs will eventually outgrow it rather sooner than later. Therefore we should stabilise updating reference values inside `appState` & `elements`, so that we can always perform quick reference checks and go possibly infinitely deep.

At the same time, we should reconsider using an array as part of reference values, as if we would like to depend on a meaningful order, we would have to maintain a fractional index-like solution on every such value, once applying the incremental change more granularly (without overriding whole array, as we currently do i.e. with `linearElement.points`,  `element.groupIds`). 

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Enforce immutable handling of reference values to create new instances on change (not individual elements per se - we can keep mutating, but we need to be immutable on their properties).
- [ ] Ensure we don't create a new instance when there is no change (i.e. `selectedElementIds`).
- [ ] Update the diffing algo to support deep diffing based on the quick reference checks.
- [ ] Identify observed arrays (such as `element.groupIds`) which depend on the correct order and consider rewriting them into key-value records, so that they don't depend on the order (or introduce fractional-like index within).
- [ ] Consider post processing most of the reference values as part of delta calculation, so that when applying changes, we could be more granular and apply just changes (as we do with `boundElements`), not overriding whole object/array.
- [ ] Consider adding a decorator for reference values which should be post-processed, as for some it does not have to make sense (i.e. `linearElement.points`).
```
author: Mrazator

title: Simplify conflict resolution when applying increments
body:
When applying increments, we need to resolve conflicts, in case our increment modifies a bi-directional relation, which was in the meantime deleted by another client.

For now, we are resolving conflicts between bound elements, especially arrow <=> bindable elements and text <=> container. For details check the history test cases inside #7348. The simplified logic is as follows:

1. don't allow bindings pointing to deleted elements from non-deleted elements, as they could result in rendering issues
2. leave the binding on the deleted elements, so that we can restore the bi-directional bindings when restoring deleted elements through the history
3. after applying increments, always re-check if bindings are bi-directional and if they are not, try to fix them (either rebind if possible - element did not get bound to something else, or unbind from both sides)
4. when 3. results in some updates, apply such updates to the increment so that they don't get lost

```[tasklist]
- [ ] Simplify resolution of text <=> container bindings by extracting `text` from the `boundElements` into a separate property (i.e. `boundTextId`)
- [ ] Replace `boundElements` array with an object, as it does not depend on the order.
- [ ] Consider maintaining conflict-free unidirectional format in the data structure, while i.e. maintaining an in-memory index of bi-directional bindings. Might simplify conflict resolution, but could lead to the inability to restore bindings in certain situations.
- [ ] Consider having a semantic runtime validation of bindings nothing non-deleted points to deleted, non-deleted to non-deleted is always bidirectional, deleted to non-deleted is always unidirectional, etc.)
```
author: Mrazator

title: `History` improvements
body:
Similarly to #7914, this initiative encapsulates follow-ups for `History` related functionalities after a large rewrite in #7348.



```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7944
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7936
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7937
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/1473
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7948
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7933
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7938
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7934
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7924
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7940
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5714
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5437
```
author: Mrazator

title: Keep appState in sync with elements
body:
> Not solely related to `History` component, but it's indirectly causing empty undos / redo, so tying it here for now.

Keeping deleted elements inside (observed) `appState`, such as inside `selectedElementIds`, `selectedGroupIds`, `editingLinearElement` causes empty undos / redos when elements got deleted in the meantime, as the applying logic does not (and should not) expect to deal with such leftovers - it could get unnecessarily complex.

Instead, we should ensure that when deleting elements (explicitly with deletion, implicitly during undo/redo, on both local and remote updates) we also clean the related `appState`, such that there are no appState data pointing to deleted elements (both soft deleted with `isDeleted` flag and force deleted from the scene).

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Cleanup related (observed) `appState` on any type of element deletion
- [ ] Consider doing it generically for any element-related `appState`, potentially a part in the form of a subscription/dependency
```
author: Mrazator

title: Persist undo / redo stacks between sessions
body:
Partly related to #7928, though we might consider doing this much sooner. The motivation is not to lose undo/redo steps due to unintentional reloads and local data recovery possibilities. However, there are negative impacts to consider as well, which include:

- grow-only in-memory stacks might eventually be hitting memory limits (lazy-loading of certain depth might be necessary)
- users preferring to prune memory (due to memory consumption above, in case of unexpected issues caused by applying increments, etc.)
- invalidating stacks on new deployments could still lead to loss of data, as users now expect the stack to stay between sessions - unless we could perform migrations as stated in #7926
- browsers are in charge and could still eventually prune the data

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Based on the pros and cons, consider persisting the stacks in a lightweight form of #7928 
- [ ] Consider the idea of persisting the stacks for the same users but between different devices
```
author: Mrazator

title: Prevent default for Ctrl+Z when inside WYSIWYG
body: While in WYSIWYG, Ctrl+Z with an intention to undo in certain situations triggers default browser behavior - reopening the last closed tab.
author: Mrazator

title: Use Esc to cancel and capture currect selections
body:
```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Cancel currently selected elements on ESC.
- [ ] Capture this change by the store so it could be undone/redone through the history.
```
author: Mrazator

title: Capture increments when moving element with arrows
body:
```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Similar to drag, capture changes in elements' position while moving them with arrows (debounced).
```
author: Mrazator

title: Unify `selectedElementIds` and `selectedLinearElement`
body: We are keeping these in sync and doing some workarounds inside and outside `Store`, it would be better if both would be unified under one property or in theory synced automatically (as a dependency).
author: Mrazator

title: Revisit undo/redo with different `actionFinalize` cases
body:
At the moment `actionFinalize` takes care of too many use cases (linear element, invisibly small elements, freedraw, etc.), resulting in subpar experience on some history actions (empty undos, dissappearring linear element handles due to `selectedElementId`, etc.).

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Revisit all the cases `actionFinalize` is doing
- [ ] Considering handling each case separetely with it's own `storeAction`
- [ ] Especially for invisibly small elements, consider if related cases are handled well by the store
```
author: Mrazator

title: Refactor `Ordered` type away
body:
`Ordered` type was introduced as part of the #7359 to distinguish between elements with a fractional index and those without. Based on the index we could perform reconciliation and order elements within the history actions.

But, in the ideal world, all elements within the scene should be ordered. The elements on the boundaries (to be inserted into the `Scene`) might or might not be ordered - locally inserted through the `updateScene`, remotely received elements, etc.

1. Try to refactor it away, and let the `ExcalidrawElement` be already ordered.
2. Ensure to emit element arrays with valid indices through the editor APIs, such as `onChange`, `restoreElements`, `getSceneElements*` etc.
3. Don't force the host apps to pass ordered elements inside the editor (i.e. within `updateScene`, `convertToExcalidrawElements`, etc.) as fractional indices are internal behavior (also due to non-breaking changes policy).
4. Consider adding `NonRestoredExcalidrawElement` for yet unrestored elements (note that it does not cover all cases of elements on the boundaries)
author: Mrazator

title: Introduce internal transaction-like API for element mutations
body:
Within a certain action, we should have UoW-like possibilities for doing everything (in a transaction) or doing nothing (rollback to the previous state), so that we don't end up in an inconsistent state in between mutations.

1. Either perform all mutations or none (perform a rollback)
2. Don't allow mutating one element multiple times within the same transaction (~limit unnecessary version bumps)

The first step towards mimicking such behavior was done in #7348 as part of the `applyTo` logic, where either all increments are applied or none (while rolling back to `prevElements`). Check the details inside: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/c1a3b89856acc0480e9a51f9943329857293fff3/packages/excalidraw/change.ts#L1069
author: Mrazator

title: Introduce performance related tests
body:
Introduce performance measures, so that we can keep pushing forward and ensure no regression in the future, especially in the hot paths. 

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Establish benchmarking infrastructure for unit/integration tests
- [ ] Analyse options on how to measure and detect complexity for certain hot paths
- [ ] Analyse options for testing on large data sets for certain hot paths
- [ ] Analyse options of fuzz testing for certain how paths
- [ ] Gradually unify performance across various user actions to keep as stable FPS as possible
```
author: Mrazator

title: Add threshold for accidental x/y movements
body: Unprecise clicks with the touchpad or mouse could lead to accidentally capturing such changes, messing up the position of elements on history actions. Let's consider adding a good enough threshold for capturing such changes.

Excalidraw+ also has the concept of a workspace in which multiple users can collaborate.

However, there is scenario where 2 people from a different workspace would like to more together.
In that case, currently, one person can have the scene in his workspace, but the other one would just work on the shared url.
Without being able to "federate" it inside his workspace.

Have a way for the second person to have it inside his workspace would be nice.

title: Feature Request Excalidraw+ - Allow a workspace or collection between different billing accounts
body:
My understanding of the billing setup today in Excalidraw+, assumes there is one entity billed for all users in the workspace.
This makes sense for scenario where a company pays for all the users.

However, there is scenario where Excalidraw+ is used by different individuals who want to collaborate. But paid respectively by all of them. It would be nice in such a scenario, as those people are working together often to either have a common workspace or at least a common collection.
So that members of that workspace or collection can see all the scenes in there.
Even if they are not all on same billing account. Just Excalidraw+ customers.

author: justise

title: FR - drag to create a fixed width text element
body:
When creating a new text element, we'd like to be able to drag as if we're creating a rectangle. On pointer up, the dragged width is going to be the fixed width of the new text element and text extending beyond the width will be wrapped. The fixed width can be altered on future resizes.

To make this work, we'd want/need the following
- show the selection border for a text that's being rendered (this would give the user a good sense of the area in which texts can go into)
- `handleTextOnPointerDown` should be changed to fire only on pointer up if there wasn't a "pointer down -> drag" sequence

title: FR - set an automatic width when pasting a long text

title: rewrite `pointerDownState.originalElements`
body:
`pointerDownState.originalElements` is unreliable (sometimes the elements we operate on aren't set due to a race condition I can't reproduce, but we get errors for in Sentry), and inefficient (we clone every element in the scene every pointerdown).

We should make the `originalElements` into a cache that's populated with the cloned element on first `get()`.
author: dwelle

title: Click to create text inside shape
body:
Idea to improve: 
When creating a new shape (rectangle, ellipse, diamond), it is possible to create a text within this shape by double-clicking inside. This text element is then connected to the shape, so that if the shape is dragged around, the text is as well. This only works if the click happens within the center of the shape. If it's on any other position inside, the text will be created as a separate element. This is only noticeable once the shape element is being moved. Perhaps consider that any clicks within the shape would add a connected text element?

![Unbenannt](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/55084543/3bfde1a0-66da-4817-be92-2a7d8605c472)

title: Feat: Easy Text Box Without Border
body:
I often have a need to create text boxes for long text explanations. This is so that the text wraps automatically.

When having multiple text boxes on the whiteboard the view quickly becomes very busy if all of these text boxes have a border.

For a less cluttered view I remove the border from the text box, but this is a pain because:
1. You need to click on the text box and then select a transparent stroke
2. The text disappears as well when you do this
3. You then need to click on the text box again (without losing it, because it's invisible now!) and then hit enter to select the text
4. You can then set the colour or the text to the preferred colour

It would be great if there was an easier way to make a text box with an invisible border.

title: Excalidraw is great but without shortcuts for changing colors it is absolutely unbearable.
body:
Hey, I've been using excalidraw for a while now and the thing is it's such a neat and amazing plugin, I couldn't ever imagining not having it. But this post IS an issue so let's get into that. Excalidraw needs color stroke shortcuts for at least 5 of the layout shortcuts because having to navigate your mouse to the edit button, clicking, navigating to the color strokes, and clicking your desired color stroke doesn't sound that bad but it's very VERY inneficient if you have to change your color every so often. 

I request for there to be a minimum of 5 color shortcuts representing our color palette. This is a to-DIE for feature. This might not be that bad to those who draw using excalidraw but for people who make mind maps with it like me it's very frustrating having to keep doing that over and over again. It's unbearable. I would really appreciate a response on this.

The hot key for it would probably be something like alt+1 and so on or potentially something else. Excalidraw has some really good shortcuts but it's definitely very lackluster when it comes to colors and I think a lot of us feel that way.

Thanks for reading and I hope you implement this feature.

author: togglebyte

title: allow `props.initialData` to be function
body:
Right now, [`initialData`](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/initialdata) can be either a `ExcalidrawInitialDataState` or `Promise<ExcalidrawInitialDataState>`. This makes async initialization hard as you need to implement your own [`ResolvablePromise`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/utils.ts#L540-L556) abstraction.

We should also allow `initialData` to be an initializer function (`() => MaybePromise<ExcalidrawInitialDataState | null>`) similar to `useState(function)`. It will be called only once so host apps don't have to worry about multiple invocations.

title: Can't change Shape Text Color from Color Picker
body:
### Problem

It is currently possible to change a shape's text color independently of its stroke color by entering text edit mode on the shape, then choosing from the default stroke colors. If the color picker is clicked, this exits text editing mode and the color is not applied.

### Action Steps:

1. Create Shape
2. Press Enter, then add text
3. Escape to select Shape
4. Change Stroke Color
This changes both the Shape and Text Color
5. Press Enter to edit text
6. Change Stroke Color from default options 
This changes the text color only which allows the Shape Stroke and the Text color to be different colors
7. While in the text editing mode, if the Stroke Color Picker is clicked, the user is removed from text editing mode and the chosen color is not applied to text.

### Potential Solutions

- Add text color to the shape properties similar to Font Size and Text Align.
- Fix the text editing mode so that it does not exit text editing mode when the color picker is clicked.

author: ursasi

title: Publishing the container image with tags in addition to "latest"
body:
Would it be possible to publish the container image with tags in addition to just `latest`?

Either using the version of excalidraw that is included or just a date (20240611)? That would allow referring to the image versions without using the full-fledged sha256sum hash...

I daresay this needs just some little tweaks to the workflow:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/.github/workflows/publish-docker.yml#L25

There is an action that does a lot of the heavy lifting and collects the metadata:

```
      # This step uses
      # [docker/metadata-action](https://github.com/docker/metadata-action#about)
      # to extract tags and labels that will be applied to the specified image.
      # The `id` "meta" allows the output of this step to be referenced in a
      # subsequent step. The `images` value provides the base name for the tags
      # and labels.
      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
```

https://docs.github.com/en/actions/publishing-packages/publishing-docker-images

Kind Regards,
Johannes

author: zsviczian

title: Complex Drawing Poor Performance
body:
Been using this amazing app for a while and when started experimenting drawing 2D CAD Sketches of certain Architectures here on Excalidraw end up faced with a problem in terms of performance, which led me to explore a little deeper.

Now I understand the whole concept of this project, I totally get that the most important thing is maintaining the simplicity (therefore most users may not need this much complexity). However my case is the following: In a good enough time horizon, users may have big canvasses (due to only using one for everything, at least thats what I see happening/experience) so whether they want it or not this complexity may collapse over time.

## Benchmarks (on M1 Macbook Air 16GB)

Overall Performance gets really bad when a lot of elements on the viewport, even unusable at a certain point (The screen goes blinking)

### On Chrome
- From 4k-8k Object performs quite well, when it gets to 8k starts to struggle
- From 10-14k Objects noticed the FPS dropped significantly (~30FPS)
![Screenshot 2024-06-14 at 01 43 43](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/103663493/f6e587e0-e61a-4f7c-b429-55fd1a39e21e)


### On Firefox
- From 14k-24k Objects the canvas gets unusable (The screen goes blinking) and it is almost impossible to move items
![Screenshot 2024-06-14 at 01 44 06](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/103663493/93037907-40bf-4110-a94d-06ede54c03bc)


- Also one thing I noticed on both of these scenarios was that **Mutating and Managing Objects** gets really slow (~10FPS)
> Since the testing files were too big I left them on a **[repo here](https://github.com/jorgedanisc/excalidraw-performance-testing)**

# Possible Solutions

## Rendering

- Leveraging WebGL directly or through a library like **[Pixi.js](https://github.com/pixijs/pixijs)** could boost rendering performance, as I tested on this **[example](https://github.com/AZhur771/excalidraw-webgl)** when someone tried to do it.
- From my research there as been a lot of effort on the rendering side from the team, here is what I noted:
    - Excalidraw already uses 2 Canvas: One for interactability and another as an Background Canvas as of my understanding. This seems to help out a lot in performance, however I do not understand how **mutating objects with a complex canvas** is a bit slow considering this.
    - From what I can see **`culling`**  seems to be already implemented

> Just as reference, found this [interesting showcase](https://antv.vision/infinite-canvas-tutorial/guide/lesson-008) on optimizing `<canvas/>` performance on the web
> 

I believe this problem is not just tied to rendering, therefore I laid out other possible solutions bellow.

## Saving and Storage

- Using Binary instead of JSON for big files:  **[FlatBuffers](https://github.com/google/flatbuffers)** due to the file being way too big with many objects, this way we can use bulk saving still (overwriting the canvas file on local storage), however now we could do it on the IndexedDB (~500MB max) by saving there the FlatBuffer Binary. This wouldn’t change much infrastructure because the AppState/Data Structures of the canvas remain the same, the thing changing would be the export/save and open mechanisms.
    - This would also significantly reduce the `.excalidraw` file size, and if necessary, FlatBuffers support JSON parsing for readability or debugging proposes.
    - FlatBuffers are extremely fast and I believe they could be fitting in this Live Collaboration, fast Canvas Editing scenario.

## Offloading to WebWorkers (If not implemented already)

- Leveraging Parallel Processing with Web Workers to manage intensive computations can free up the main thread, allowing for smoother interaction and rendering. Tasks like real-time rendering calculations or batch processing updates could benefit significantly from this approach.

### Adapting Data Structures (would perhaps be too much overhead)

But just to leave it here if not implemented already: 

- Combine already used on `Scene` structs like `Map` and `WeakMap` with data structures such as spatial indexing (e.g., quadtrees) to manage object interactions and visibility efficiently.
- Employ object pooling to reuse object instances and minimize garbage collection overhead.

Hope the team takes my possible solutions with a grain of salt, I have not worked on the project neither have knowledge of every single details of the implementation, though I research the codebase and the app (testing) and I am just trying to help out. Could be totally wrong here, but just wanted to raise this issue without being “empty handed”.

title: Firefox for Android: Consistently freezes entire browser after a few seconds of use.
body:
I have been trying to use Excalidraw on my phone, but it invariably freezes after a few seconds of use. It doesn't matter what I'm doing; I can be typing, I can be scrolling, anything. I have to forcefully terminate Firefox, then re-open it and close the Excalidraw tab in order to prevent it from freezing again.

Edit: New insight, as I have posted in a reply below.

> If I reset the canvas (something I hadn't done before), the issue does not reoccur on Android until I zoom out. So I think the issue might have something to do with memory consumption after zooming out (it probably allocates more memory on an as-needed basis for resizes).

---
Phone model: Galaxy S21 5G (SC-51B)
Android version: 14
Firefox version: 127.0

title: Using Excalidraw React build in a vanilla JS application

### Discussed in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/8163

Originally posted by **Favourz1** June 21, 2024 Hi, I'm using the build of Excalidraw from React in a vanilla js web application with websocket. Ideally, I'm using the WebSocket to enable real-time collaboration between many users.

**Problem:** Canvas doesn't sync together with the same elements. I'm using the same websocket implementation for a chat feature which works well, so not a websocket error.
I also tried saving the data to a useref to mimic external communication and restoring on the React app before building and it worked.
I can confirm the data is sending and receiving on both ends but for some reasons, it doesn't update the canvas with current elements on each user screen canvas connected.

An example code of my react app can be found in a [codesandbox example](https://codesandbox.io/s/favourz-excalidraw-app-2w55td?file=/src/App.js) with detailed comments
Also, an overview of my websocket and vanilla js implementation can be found in a [codesnadbox example](https://codesandbox.io/s/favourz-excalidraw-app-2w55td?file=/external-index.html) with detailed comments.
I also commented on other methods I tried in the files.

title: is this best way to export as PDF ?
body:
1. Export as PDF
2. Click "Export Image."
3. SVG to File.
4. Inkscape (download third-party app).
5. Open the SVG file.
6. Export as a PDF file.

- (Note: Inkscape does not support the Excalidraw font, so you should download and update the Excalidraw font.)

I tried to find an easy way to export as a PDF file. As someone without a computer background, it was quite tough.

author: distantlullaby

title: change "hotkey" for dragging the drawing using mouse
body:
Currently, it is not possible to drag the visible drawing without using *both* hands:

- `<SPACE> + `<LCLICK>` + <MOUSE-MOVE>` will grab the canvas and move it where ever the mouse wishes.

Now, when using other drawing or mapping applications, the common "hotkeys" often look like this (or at least they can be configured accordingly):

- `<LCLICK> + <MOUSE-MOVE>` will span an area for selecting inbound elements
- `<RCLICK> + <MOUSE-MOVE>` will pan (move around) the visible area

In order to avoid frustration when having to jump from one application to another, it would be nice to be able to configure these basic mouse movements to the commonly used scheme.

Is there a place within the codebase predestined to accomplish this? At least for a test before creating a PR?

title: Excalidraw unable to reuse existing files from vault. Creates duplicate.
body:
Excalidraw doesn't detect that a file comes from the vault if drag and dropped from native file system.

Therefore, after saving, Excalidraw will create a duplicate of the image named "pasted image [numbers]" somewhere in my vault.

This can also happen if the image is stored in the same folder as the Excalidraw drawing, and if it's attachements are set to be put in the same folder. After saving, a duplicate will appear right next to the original file, and deleting that duplicate removes the image from the Excalidraw drawing.

If holding shift when drag and dropping, it'll instead use an absolute file link to the file on my drive, but this has the inconvenient of breaking whenever the images are moved to a different folder.

This can be fixed manually after the fact. After drag and dropping the file, open the Excalidraw file in Markdown mode, then in the "attachements" section where it links to all of the files used in the drawing, manually edit the link of the images to a wiki link that points to your original image.

Since it's a wiki link, it will not break upon moving the files around in the vault. Obsidian will also take care of updating the wiki link to that file if it was renamed from within the Obsidian file explorer. It will work without issues as long as the files keep a unique name across the vault.
Having to do it manually for every file takes time, and is unmanageable when trying to create large image moodboards.

Since Excalidraw already supports pointing to attachements via wiki links, this feature is an odd exclusion (?) or a bug (?)

Excalidraw does mention "reuse existing files from the vault" in the drag and drop popup, but it doesn't appear to work yet ?

author: ghost

title: labeled arrow not rendering when zoomed in on ios
body:
On ios, labeled arrow is not rendered when zoomed in. An error occurs that canvas area exceeded the maximum limit 16,777,216 (4096 * 4096)

This occurs because during rendering labeled arrow, `tempCanvas` is created but its dimension is not capped (unlike `generateElementWithCanvas()`).

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/df8875a497b2111ec8c21963d66a2e2396d15bbd/packages/excalidraw/renderer/renderElement.ts#L484

However, capping the dimension causes the rendering quality to significantly worsen when arrow becomes labelled arrow.

title: Bug on Firefox: scroll gets reset on appending text before the end of the text
body:
Firefox version: 128.0. I don't get the same issue on Chromium 127.0.6533.57.

The problem goes away when I comment [the lines setting `editable.selectionStart` and `editable.selectionEnd`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/df8875a497b2111ec8c21963d66a2e2396d15bbd/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L223-L224). The problem also goes away if the [`overflow: hidden` line](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/df8875a497b2111ec8c21963d66a2e2396d15bbd/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L298) is commented.

The following is a video showing the bug in addition to logging the values of `editable.selectionStart` and `editable.selectionEnd` below the mentioned two lines:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/12ad41e1-cb09-4a8d-82dc-62030f468770

This was a bit annoying while taking notes.

I hid the menu at the bottom since it covered the text at the bottom (but that's another issue :sweat_smile:).

The reason the scroll gets reset instead of just not scrolling is because of [the line setting `scrollTop` to 0](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/df8875a497b2111ec8c21963d66a2e2396d15bbd/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L260). If I comment it, the scrolling just stops.

title: Unable to insert images located in `~/Library/Application Support/` on macOS
body: ### Environment * macOS Version 13.6.6 * Google Chrome Version 126.0.6478.183 ### Issue I tried to insert a PNG image located in `~/Library/Application Support/` on my Mac, but Excalidraw showed a dialog saying it could not open files because the folder contained system files. You may ask why I need to insert images from a system folder. The reason is that some apps store generated images in their "Application Support" folder (`~/Library/Application Support//`). For example, [Minecraft](https://www.minecraft.net/) stores in-game screenshots inside `~/Library/Application Support/minecraft/screenshots/` automatically to simplify user interactions. In contrast to Excalidraw showing an error dialog, [tldraw](https://www.tldraw.com/) can insert files from `~/Library/Application Support/` successfully.

title: Add Option to disable Analytics
body:
While one might have the impression that contacting `scripts.simpleanalyticscdn.com` is just privacy-friendly and does not require user-consent, one might want to disable this completely to ensure the trust of more skeptical users.

Sending any data to some other domain might be considered a GDPR violation (see also the extreme example of [Google Fonts](https://runcloud.io/blog/google-fonts-gdpr)).

So I would like to have this disabled as well when using `VITE_APP_ENABLE_TRACKING=False` (which I would have expected to be opt-in due to the renaming, but the default seems to be True as well - which could also be changed?

author: mtolmacs

title: Fonts no longer load
body:
When I am using an adblocker that blocks requests to Google domains, the fonts no longer load, and everything is in a serif font.
It looks like this is because the fonts are being loaded from Google Fonts domains.

This didn't use to be a problem until the last week or so.

Possible solutions:
- Vendor the fonts (i.e., self-host them on your domain). This means that adblockers will allow them to load, even if only the three default fonts are vendored.
- Include a keyword like `cursive`, `sans-serif` or `monospace` in the `font-family` for the three default fonts. This would mean that even if external fonts are blocked, it could fall back to a system font that's close enough.

Ideally, both proposed solutions would be implemented so that even if the self-hosted fonts don't load, a reasonable fallback is available.


Browser: Firefox 128 (with uBlock origin configured to block requests to Google domains)
OS: macOS 15 (beta) 

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c79b778a-e982-454f-aef9-686708b4c950)

title: export to blob does not load new fonts
body: Export to blob outside the editor (`exportToBlob`, but also `exportToCanvas`), does not load new fonts, as the fonts are not loaded by the `FontFace` API. This does not affect SVG export (as the fonts are fetched manually), nor PNG export inside the editor (as by coincidence fonts are already loaded by the editor), but it does affect `link.excalidraw.com` (i.e. https://app.excalidraw.com/l/6rfy1007QOo/7YLPfH1nMfU). In the following screenshot, we should see `Excalifont`, but instead we see a fallback font.

author: Piyushpatil03

title: Reduce exported SVG size by glyph subsetting
body:
ATM we embed whole fonts as data URLs (#8012), which increases the size of the exported SVGs.

Instead, we should only inline the glyphs which are used.

**Additional context**
- consider adding an option to skip font inlining completely - this could be useful when having multiple SVGs in one place, leaving the font definition on the host environment

**Technical design**
- we shall embed only relevant unicode ranges (right now all ranges are blindly inlined)
- ideally, we shall use a library to parse font data that works both inside and outside of the browser (for server-side export)
- in the future, we should be able to distinguish between subfamilies (regular, medium, bold, semibold)

It would be great if the library had a search box at the top so you could enter something like "postgres" and have any matching items brought to the top.

Additionally the side pane with the library is _really_ small both in frame size and object size compared to the rest of excalidraw - this means you end up scrolling vertically for a long time and squinting to see what the items are. Perhaps this could be a resizable pane and respect cmd++/- to scale the items?
author: sammcj

title: [BUG]: history.ts appears in package.
body:
I've noticed that history.ts is always included in the package when running `yarn pack` from `packages/excalidraw`. 
I took a look at the yarn source code and this is what I found in their `src/cli/commands/pack.js`:
```
const NEVER_IGNORE = ignoreLinesToRegex([
  // never ignore these files
  '!/package.json',
  '!/readme*',
  '!/+(license|licence)*',
  '!/+(changes|changelog|history)*',
]);
```
There is no way to overwrite files included by this `NEVER_IGNORE`. 

So the only fix I can think of is renaming history.ts. (or just leaving this minor bug for newer generations to stumble upon...)
author: s1lverfish

title: text-related `history` issues
body:
There are several history issues related to text elements / wysiwyg (though some potentially unrelated).

### undo stack not populated (consistently)

1. reload
2. single click (with selection tool) on canvas
3. pick text tool using keyboard (`T`)
4. single click to create text tool
5. -> history not populated

If you skip step (2), history is generally populated correctly. "Generally" because sometimes it doesn't happen.

## undoing leaves empty-text element

When history is populated when creating a text element, then undoing it leaves you with empty `element.text` element. It shouldn't keep empty-text elements as we auto-delete them if you go the wysiwyg path.

1. create text element so that it populates history
2. confirm
3. undo (twice depending if the history now also contains deselection)
4. results in empty text element

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61e16733-3302-4895-b64c-54ba388a496b)

author: Mrazator

title: Comic Shanns related issues
body: Consider the following comparison between Cascadia and Comic Shanns. It's apparent Comic Shanns isn't truly mono-spaced. ![cascadia-comic shanns](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8bf15f84-c79c-4adf-8771-d8ceb27a2c4b) ``` Sphinx of Black Quartz, Judge My Vow!!! Sphinx of Black Quartz, Judge My Vow!!! Sphinx of Black Quartz, Judge My Vow!!! 12345678901234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789 [ ] In a constant-width font, the 117 spaces between the [] above would make the line start/end points align perfectly! ``` The numbers alignment seems to be way off as well. E.g.: Extended Latin glyphs like `Č, Ř, Ě` are incorrect. ![Untitled-2024-08-05-2003](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c21eed1a-c97d-4dab-a533-a8539b009e32) Some other glyphs are incorrect, like "¿" - "questiondown" : ![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98bb9207-f2d2-4ed6-9cd8-f5dad58ea3a2) Could we find an OFL-like licensed fork solving most of these issues? E.g.: 1. https://github.com/dtinth/comic-mono-font 2. https://github.com/shannpersand/comic-shanns 3. https://github.com/kaBeech/serious-shanns 4. https://github.com/jesusmgg/comic-shanns-mono --- Once we find the right alternative, let's also: - split all the fonts into multiple chunks - update preloads which include the hashes rn - separate fonts into a `FontFamilyName` subfolder

title: feat: Improve fast flowchart creation
body:
Building on the functionality described in #8329, I would like to request the ability to specify the type of symbol to be created while holding down the Ctrl key.

Example:
I have a square node selected, and I want to create a diamond next. By pressing `Ctrl + Arrow Key + 3`, I would create a diamond.
To create another square, I would press `Ctrl + Arrow Key + 2`.
If I press `Ctrl + Arrow Key` again, a square is created since the current node is a square.
This enhancement would provide more flexibility in designing flowcharts by allowing users to directly control the type of node they create while maintaining the streamlined workflow provided by the existing keyboard shortcuts.

title: Going over the local storage limit while using Excalidraw can lead to unintended data loss
body:
- Multiple users reporting that creating larger boards on excalidraw.com sometimes stop saving for them. This is due to excalidraw.com currently not reporting real-time available storage to the user.
- Some users report losing their entire board unexpectedly after leaving the excalidraw.com domain. This could be made more robust by reading back the stored data from local storage after saving and checking against the board in memory via a quick hash compare
- Few people report losing their board after cleaning their local storage, maybe making it more clear for first time users on the Welcome page that they cannot clean their local storage if they want to keep their board.
  - Alternative idea is asking the user if we should notify for download before navigating away, i.e. is the user trying to do something important or just trying it out.

author: zhangwei900808

title: First-class fonts support for CJK
body:
In the first wave, we should add built-in support for Chinese (CJK*), after that, we could experiment with different languages (Japanese, Korean, Indian, Arabic, Hebrew, etc.) and their specifics (baseline alignments, text direction, substitutions, positioning, etc.).

> *After all we will be adding in the first wave support for the whole CJK as proposed in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/8530

First things first, conceptually we should consider the following.

1. Chinese fonts are huge, but we cannot rely on system fonts for inconsistency between OSes, hence
   - they should be lazy loaded based on the used codepoints (~separation into independent FontFaces)
   - we should embed just the used glyphs inside SVGs, not the whole font (~only browser options seems to be Harfbuzz wasm + consider quick exist based on specified unicode range)
   - we should likely have a font family per each built-in family, to match their style (~one option is patching per each family as suggested in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5370#issuecomment-1181272316)
     -  could we perhaps adjust the glyphs based on the base font? (looks like not yet)
   - we should split the font into multiple font faces (based on our harfbuzz or a more elaborate splitter, like https://chinese-font.netlify.app/online-split)
     - consider splitting our built in fonts as well
2. Chinese codepoints could overlap with Japanese
   - we could normalize codepoints between languages or use a base font which does it already (Noto Sans CJK?)
   - we might dynamically specify the order of fallback fonts based on the selected (prefer Japanese over Chinese when Japanese is the app language but it could be fragile)
   - speaking of Japanese, something to keep in mind is that they combine different writing systems (kanji, hiragana, katakana)
3. Chinese has different variants (traditional / hong-kong, simplified), with top-down / left-right directions
   - there are even more combinations of writing system / language
   - we should likely aim at the simplified left-right variant first
   
4. Wrapping algo might need to be tweaked to support Chinese fully
5. Vertical offset might need to be adjusted to eliminate LS

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/8300
- [ ] https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/8258
```

title: Handwriting experience does not feel smooth on iPad
body:
Hello, I like the concept of ExcaliDraw, and have tried it multiple times because I'm hoping that it'll help me accomplish what I want with handwritten notes in Obsidian. However, the handwriting experience always puts me off. 
- I would like the handwriting to feel smooth, with minimal lag between my pen and the ink showing on the screen. 
- I would also personally prefer a pen type that does not have pressure sensitivity so that each stroke is uniform. 

I've added a video to show the difference between writing in ExcaliDraw (on the website version) vs. another note-taking app on the iPad, called Nebo App. Unfortunately, the video is not able to capture the true difference in the feel because you cannot see my pen nib.  

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f40f5071-2334-4e65-8b8d-5280eba62328
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e59167a7-7ab9-4de9-94b8-d2b3da51a60b

The reason I chose Nebo App as the comparison is because their developer, MyScript, has an API that can be used. Their handwriting recognition is awesome. I'm not a developer, so I'm not entirely sure if it can be used for ExcaliDraw, but I would suggest looking into it! https://github.com/MyScript

title: Forking Excalidraw to customise UI and use our own collab server as package
body:
Folks, 

I forked Excalidraw and built the package with different name which also includes Collaboration by updating the entryPoints as suggested [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/3879#discussioncomment-10213119), I also changed the served endpoints

Once you update the entryPoints, [main field in package.json in packages/excalidraw](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/package.json#L4) also need to be changed to `App.js`, which i did.

Now i published the package with a different name @abc/excalidraw - All good till now.

When i try to use the @abc/excalidraw in my other app - 

- There are [internal dependencies ](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/excalidraw-app/package.json#L29) which are not found in package, How to navigate this ? Do we need to install it 

- `import { Excalidraw } from '@abc/excalidraw` doesn;t work - It has to be `import Excalidraw from @abc/excalidraw`

- Collaboration doesn't work, because https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/tree/master/excalidraw-app contains some environment variables, how are we supposed to pass env var to package ?

i.e - When i start Collab, it says FIREBASE_CONFIG not found, how am i supposed to pass these var to package?
author: mbj36

title: Importing Mermaid diagram with markdown text syntax does not seem to support auto-wrap behavior
body:
I'm trying to import a relatively simple Mermaid diagram into excalidraw:

```mermaid
---
title: Connect Four Gameplay
---
flowchart
	Start(["`Start`"])
	Initialize["`Initialize the game`"]
	NextToken["`Get the next player's token`"]
	DropToken["`Drop the token into the first unoccupied spot from the bottom of the column`"]
	IsWinningMove?{"`Did the current player make the winning move?`"}
	HaveOpenSpaces?{"`Are there open spaces left?`"}
	GameOverWinner["`The current player wins`"]
	GameOverDraw["`It's a draw`"]
	Stop(["`Stop`"])

	Start-->Initialize-->NextToken
	NextToken-->DropToken-->IsWinningMove?
	IsWinningMove?-->|Yes|GameOverWinner-->Stop
	IsWinningMove?-->|No|HaveOpenSpaces?
	HaveOpenSpaces?-->|No|GameOverDraw-->Stop
	HaveOpenSpaces?-->|Yes|NextToken
```

I'm using the markdown syntax for dealing with text because it also auto-wraps text, at least when I use it in Obsidian.

When I try to import this into excalidraw, it effectively kills any wrapping. Maybe there's another issue at play, but it seems like the text most nodes that have whitespace in its text get collapsed and the nodes themselves are rendered with just a character width worth of space.

https://ibb.co/kDp3F1k

title: Inconsistent behavior when locking elements inside frames
body:
Frames and the ability to lock elements don't seem to work well together:

- When you lock an element inside a frame, you can no longer access its `Unlock` option when you right-click on the element while it's still inside the frame.
  - Note that if you reduce the size of the frame via its handle so as to remove the element from the frame, you will again have access to its `Unlock` option. 
- When any element is locked within a frame, selecting `Lock all` option from the frame's context menu will unlock every previously locked elements inside it

title: Rectangular Arrows Alignment
body:
I am requesting a feature to allow the rectangular arrows to be moved left-right and up-down, so they don't overlap each other. This will improve the user experience and make the interface more intuitive.

The current implementation of the arrows causes them to overlap when placed close together. This can be confusing for users and makes it difficult to distinguish between different elements. Allowing the arrows to be positioned more precisely will enhance the overall design and functionality of the project.

Example:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19aad213-f5f1-4701-a41a-65e392089090)


This feature would be useful in various scenarios where precise alignment of arrows is required. It could be particularly helpful in applications such as flowcharts, diagrams, or any other visual representation that uses arrows for direction or connection.


The wanted example could be this:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9adb7565-8e8b-48ba-8088-f5df805d9c47)

title: Buffer not supported on mobile devices - ExcalidrawFonts
body:
@Mrazator

Buffer class is a part of Node.js, not standard JavaScript that runs in browsers (or WebView). Should Buffer be used here?
This causes error with Excalidraw on Obsidian Mobile:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/51ea184938d57637faa345de85028aa8a0930ed2/packages/excalidraw/fonts/ExcalidrawFont.ts#L52-L55

In the other case there is a check around it.

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/51ea184938d57637faa345de85028aa8a0930ed2/packages/excalidraw/fonts/ExcalidrawFont.ts#L137-L142
author: zsviczian

title: Arrow does not update both sides when moving connected object
body:
When an arrow connects 2 objects (A, B), and one of the two is moved, only the point connected directly to the moving object is updated. The other point remains fixed until the other object itself is moved.
1. Arrow points from A to B:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aed4ec73-9a25-45bd-bd72-a04c2cd6268b)
2. A is moved and only the arrow's starting point updates:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b76b68fc-35a5-43ba-82f1-00682627ae96)
3. Only once B itself moves (even just by 1 pixel), the arrow's point connected to B updates immediately and snaps back into place:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6bc3e286-de86-4be4-af01-45fccc3ce044)

I remember that both sides of the arrow were consistently updated not long ago.
author: Vortex-I

title: Stuck in zoom/pan mode after Wacom pen click
body:
Same behaviour as #3553

I triggered this bug while drawing on https://excalidraw.com/
Chrome browser. M1 Macbook Pro 2021, Macos 14.6.1. Wacom Intuos BT M on USB. Tablet driver 6.4.4-5. Stylus works properly, pressure etc.

To reproduce:

- Open new drawing in a fresh browser window
- Use the pen tool (7) and start drawing with Wacom tablet
- While Wacom stylus is still on the tablet, press the front stylus button (scroll)
- Lift the pen from the tablet
- Release the stylus button

The first new state is a kind of stuck drawing state, where the stylus keeps drawing even on hover (or mouse move).
One tap or click fixes this, but now it is in the previously reported zoom/pan mode.
Any attempt to draw with a mouse drag or stylus drag results in a pan, or a combination zoom and pan, depending on the exact tool selected. Drawing is not possible at all.

A page refresh cures it completely.

It doesn't happen on Safari, only Chrome.
To be fair, other applications like GIMP also are broken by Wacom button press, lift, drag.

author: Joey-Mars

title: Typescript types are not available in @next version
body:
Currently, I'm using the last stable version of Excalidraw NPM package v0.17.3 in my project.
This works great, thank you for this wonderful project.

The problem happens when I try **@next** version of the package which seams lacks any TypeScript types which makes it unusable in TypeScript project.

Before, I could do something like this:

```ts
import {
  AppState,
  BinaryFiles,
  ExcalidrawImperativeAPI,
  ToolType
} from '@excalidraw/excalidraw/types/types';
```

But this is not working anymore.

I have a suspicion that the issue was introduced in the following commit: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/commit/a8064ba3eef10fa50bf5d9f402b779d54558ae76#

If yo look at `package.json` file changes you will see this:

Before:

```json
"files": [
     "dist/*",
     "types/*"
   ],
```

After:

```json
"files": [
     "dist/*"
   ],
```

Is this intentional?

How can we import types now?

title: Excalidraw Plus: Presentation export in PDF or PPTX is set to 4:3 ratio, presentation frames are 16:9 default.
body:
**App:** Excalidraw Plus on https://app.excalidraw.com, September 17th 2024

**Issue**: PDF and Powerpoint export of presentations downloads in an unexpected / unconfigurable ratio.

When using frames to develp a presentation, it looks like the default frame is approx. 16:9 ratio. This presents well on the equivalent screen when using presentation mode.

When exporting either a PDF or PPTX you are provide with a file in a 4:3 ratio meaning content layout has whitespace at header and footer.

This can be worked around in the PPTX with a resize but is baked in for the PDF without an editor.

May have missed it (newbie!) but there doesn't seem to be a way to configure the export for either the PDF or PPTX target.

Thanks for an awesome app!

title: Allow changing shape type of already on canvas elements preserving bindings
body:
For large and complex diagrams with multiple interconnected bindings it would be great to allow changing the shape type on-the-fly, instead of remove, recreate and connect linear elements.

It should support changing by keyboard shortcut as well, maybe `Ctrl/Cmd + number`, matching the number keys for current tools.

It should be limited to meaningful origin and target combinations, specifically rectangle, diamond, oval, image only.

author: mtolmacs

title: Quick switch between shapes (rect <-> circle, etc.)
body:
Add the ability to switch between different shapes quickly, keeping the original shape style and bound text size.

The current way of doing this includes a lot of manual steps:

- turn on snapping and create a new shape of a similar size
- copy the styles from the original shape and paste the style into the new shape
- unbind text from the original shape and bind it to the new shape (usually the size of the bound text is different than the built-in size)

Instead, changing from rect <-> circle/diamond should be doable with a single user action, for all the selected elements.

Related discussion #8277

Is there any way to undo and get back the original design?

title: How to publish packages/excalidraw to npmjs.com
body:
**What I want to do ?**
I want to publish custom packages/excalidraw to the npm site, and then I need to integrate it into the application system. Now I don't know how to publish it to the npm site?

I have tried the following methods, but cannot get my answer.

**Method 1st:**
1. fork project

2. execute following script:

  ```
  rm yarn.lock
  ```  

3. /home/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw to execute

  ```
  1. yarn gen:types
     yarn build.esm
     yarn pack
  
  "scripts": {
  "gen:types": "rm -rf types && tsc",
  "build:esm": "rm -rf dist && node ../../scripts/buildPackage.js && yarn gen:types",
  "pack": "yarn build:umd && yarn pack",
  "start": "node ../../scripts/buildExample.mjs && vite",
  "build:example": "node ../../scripts/buildExample.mjs",
  "size": "yarn build:umd && size-limit"
  }
  ```

  

4. npm login, npm publish will publish well

**However, the file structure of the published npm package is different from the file structure of the npm package published on the official website.**
https://www.npmjs.com/package/@excalidraw/excalidraw?activeTab=code

https://www.npmjs.com/package/excalidraw-zh?activeTab=code



**method 2nd:**
1. fork project

2. update \packages\excalidraw\package.json  key=name

3. update .github\workflows\autorelease-excalidraw.yml to workflow_dispatch: to Allow manual submission

4. add some comments in files

5. run action autorelease-excalidraw.yml will happen the following errors:

```
dependency "react@^16.8.0 || ^17.0.0 || ^18.0.0".
error Command failed with exit code 2.
error Command failed with exit code 2.
Error: Command failed: yarn run build:esm

author: Forbrave

title: Elbow arrow binding jumps to highest element.
body:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2cbdc76c-4bbc-4497-8c0a-2f6806184462

I think the video demonstrates the problem quite well. This problem only occurs if the other element is higher than the one it was previously bound to.

I tried fixing the problem and I found that this isn't only appearing for elbow arrows, there is similar unexpected behavior with all arrows:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a2f5573b-67e7-4a23-b4ab-dcd24d39e811

In this case it seems the elements order doesn't matter.

For the elbow arrow problem I think it originates from the way `isDragging` is handled in `updateElbowArrow` (in `element/routing.ts`). If the arrow is being dragged it doesn't look at which point is being dragged and tries to snap both ends, even if one was already bound to something else and is not being moved.

author: Cmoen11

title: Add or remove selected objects to/from a specific(selected) group
body:
- There's no way to remove a selected object from a group without ungrouping all the members(destroying the group) and grouping again with the new object. If the group is a child of another group, the freed objects will get in the parent and there's no way to create a child group out of some objects of an entire group.
- We can't add an object to an entire group unless grouping the object and the group together that creates a new bigger group involving both, that is not the answer.

- The best state is to have a hierarchical structure of the groups(or all the canvas like figma) to logically move objects between groups.
- Suggested solution:
1. defining a Group type with having its individual paragraph in the JSON.
2. assigning any object to only one group instead of an array of groups
3. defining a parent ID for groups

This structure improves the process of working with groups gets us simpler and cleaner code.



```[tasklist]
### Tasks
```

author: KwiatekMiki

title: Passing a read only object as initialData throws an exception and blows up the app
body:
We use Excalidraw within our product for a canvas tool.
We recently found that some users were not able to load some of their canvases. It would not render at all.

We found that if our `initialData` had an element with `fillType` of `cross-hatch`, excalidraw would try to mutate the `points` property inside that element. It's not excalidraw itself, but a library named `hachure-fill` which does it. And the `initialData` we sent to excalidraw component was read-only (deep frozen). `apollo-graphql` does that to server responses so that their cache is read-only. We solved the problem by deep copying the object and making it mutable.

I am not even sure if should be considered an issue, but i guess it would be great to ensure that the prop `initialData` is immutable all the way through.

Here's a reproduction of the issue - https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/my5l7h

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e6eca11d-74a4-44b0-8396-e26420090366)

Please let me know if you need any more information about it.

author: RobsKanbanZone

title: Feature - Preview map
body:
Hi, 
As the size of whiteboards increase, the updates on viewport by dragging is not efficient for users. 
A `preview map` for the whole whiteboard would be really helpful. And I would like to help implement this feature.![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f9040783-5e2c-4962-af40-cf945df0db4a)


**Functional Requirements:**

1. Toggle Visibility
Open/Close Preview Map:
Users can click a designated button to open or close the preview map.
Provide a keyboard shortcut to toggle the preview map for quick access.

2. Display of Whiteboard Content
Complete Outline View:
The preview map displays outlines of all shapes and objects present on the whiteboard.
Maintain aspect ratio to prevent distortion of the whiteboard representation.
Real-time Updates:
The preview map updates in real-time to reflect additions, deletions, or modifications on the whiteboard.

3. Viewport Representation
Current Viewport Indicator:
The preview map includes a highlighted rectangle indicating the area currently visible in the main viewport.
Dynamic Updating:
As users navigate the whiteboard (e.g., pan or zoom), the viewport indicator updates correspondingly in the preview map.

4. Navigation via Preview Map
Click to Navigate:
Users can click on any area within the preview map to instantly move the main viewport to that location.
Drag to Pan:
Users can drag the viewport indicator within the preview map to pan the main viewport smoothly.

5. Performance Optimization
Efficient Rendering:
The preview map should render efficiently without causing lag, even on large or complex whiteboards.
Resource Management:
Implement optimizations to minimize CPU and memory usage.

title: 编辑器api

author: handsome-xiaowang

title: Option to disable the add text function on double tap in mobile
body: I understand this isn't a bug, but it would be great if there were an option to disable the double-tap add text function in mobile view. The current behavior isn't very user-friendly, as when I'm adjusting elements on the canvas,  an accidental double tap frequently triggers the text input mode and opens the keyboard. This disrupts the workflow and makes it harder to interact with the canvas smoothly. Disabling this feature would significantly improve the user experience on mobile devices.

author: dwelle

title: Failure to Automatically Read and Save to Existing Excalidraw Canvas File After Reload
body:
When saving an Excalidraw canvas file and reloading the webpage, the app fails to automatically read from the previously saved file. 

After reloading, users cannot save changes to the existing file. Instead, they must save to a new file or manually reopen the existing one to continue working
